<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927535 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00788: Operational Constraints

- page_id: `191927535`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927535/Operational+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:49:02.640+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

833654917

833654919

833654918

**Description**

The Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view concerns defining operational limitations, constraints and performance parameters for the enterprise. It specifies traditional textual operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. The addition of SysML parametrics provides a computational means of defining operational constraints across the enterprise or within a specific operational context.

The intended usage of the Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view includes:

- Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Definition of business rules.
- Identification of operational constraints.

**Implementation**

The Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- Operational Constraints Excel Spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Constraints table

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

833654916

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2f8a1c2-9aa0-4673-a3d4-d4e146e7d568"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833654917</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bd1bb8b-75e0-4178-9f14-457eb4531a95"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833654919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67db9afc-51b6-4d1a-b61a-58788ab3de13"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833654918</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view concerns defining operational limitations, constraints and performance parameters for the enterprise. It specifies traditional textual operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. The addition of SysML parametrics provides a computational means of defining operational constraints across the enterprise or within a specific operational context.</p><p>The intended usage of the Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.</li><li>Definition of business rules.</li><li>Identification of operational constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Operational Constraints (Op-Ct) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Constraints table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Constraints Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Operational Constraints Excel Spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational Constraints" ac:alt="Operational Constraints"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_constraints.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Constraints" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Operational Constraints table</h6><p><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p>You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the <strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p>To select a constraint owner</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Go to <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong>, and in the <strong>Default Constraint Owners</strong> property group, choose a needed owner.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfcfc4d2-9114-4843-bcde-d9ce65d0a0f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833654916</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Constraint" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Performer" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Agent" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Information Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Rule" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Subject Of Operational Constraint" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="104cff27-df0c-40e6-85ab-f5969af72d35" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929029 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00789: Operational Control Flow

- page_id: `191929029`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929029/Operational+Control+Flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:40:56.861+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Operational Activity Actions.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Control Flow

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Operational Activity Actions.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Control Flow</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929030 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00790: Operational Exchange

- page_id: `191929030`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929030/Operational+Exchange
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:40:45.561+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Information Flow

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Extensions</strong></span></p><p><span class="fontstyle2">UML Information Flow</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929031 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00791: Operational Exchange Item

- page_id: `191929031`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929031/Operational+Exchange+Item
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:40:32.135+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Operational Performers and conveyed by an Operational Exchange.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Operational Performers and conveyed by an Operational Exchange. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928156 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00792: Operational Exchange Manager dialog

- page_id: `191928156`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928156/Operational+Exchange+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:29:07.853+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['operational-exchange-manager-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog enables the analysis and management of the Operational Exchanges data. You can:

- Edit existing Operational Exchanges by adding new Operational Exchange Items
- Realize or ignore existing Operational Exchanges on selected relationships
- Change the Operational Exchange Item 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Review producing and consuming Operational Activity pairs and remove the ones that are not needed
- Review the list of diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange

To open an **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association, Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='op_ech_manager_manipulator.png'] . The Operational Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by the selected Realized Operational Exchange.
- The All Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by all the Realized Exchanges in the Operational Exchange Manager dialog.

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Internal Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in NOV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929032 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00793: Operational Information

- page_id: `191929032`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929032/Operational+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:40:20.683+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform).</p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Extensions</strong></span></p><p><span class="fontstyle2">Class</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927542 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00794: Operational Information Op-If

- page_id: `191927542`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927542/Operational+Information+Op-If
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:48:44.745+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

905585431

905585433

905585432

**Description**

The Operational Information (Op-If) view addresses the information perspective on operational architecture. It allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific issues.

The Conceptual Data Model diagram defines the high-level information elements used in the operational scenarios.

It is used for documenting the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual data modeling in the Conceptual Data Model diagram. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

The Operational Information (Op-If) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Information diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . It is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships. The intended usage of the Conceptual Data Model diagram includes: - Information requirements. - Information hierarchy.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . It supports the information engineering notation.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Conceptual Data Model diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Logical Data Model diagram

905585429

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implements' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e18da588-5b04-4613-b32a-47d068b2b7fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">905585431</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5236889a-5d28-482c-b44a-36f055ed5687"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">905585433</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d81aae16-fe8e-40f2-acd1-92205c93e1bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">905585432</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Operational Information (Op-If) view addresses the information perspective on operational architecture. It allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific issues.</p><p>The Conceptual Data Model diagram defines the high-level information elements used in the operational scenarios.</p><p>It is used for documenting the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.</p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration</strong></span></p><p><span class="fontstyle2">You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual data modeling in the Conceptual Data Model diagram. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.</span></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Operational Information (Op-If) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Information diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Conceptual Data Model diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.<br />The intended usage of the Conceptual Data Model diagram includes:<br />- Information requirements.<br />- Information hierarchy.</li></ul><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Entity Relationship Diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Relationship Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It supports the information engineering notation.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_conceptual_data_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information Op-If" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Conceptual Data Model diagram</h6><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational Information" ac:alt="Operational Information"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_information.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information Op-If" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">An example of the Logical Data Model diagram</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28da2c85-a204-4f6e-a85c-f8a40450204c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">905585429</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Information Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Implements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="66ba841b-5b64-414f-873b-ec62171481c8" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Entity Relationship Diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927607 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00800: Operational Motivation

- page_id: `191927607`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927607/Operational+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-28T16:58:35.577+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The**Operational Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and their relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The<strong> Operational Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and their relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

**Description**

The Operational Parameters (Op-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Operational Parameters (Op-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Operational Parameters (Op-Pm) domain is represented by:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927548 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00806: Operational Processes

- page_id: `191927548`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927548/Operational+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:56:59.676+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

908843947

908843949

908843948

**Description**

The Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view concerns capturing activity based behavior and flows. It describes the activities that are normally conducted in the course of achieving business goals that support a capability. It describes operational activities, their inputs/outputs, operational activity actions and flows between them.

The intended usage of the Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view includes:

- Description of activities and workflows.
- Requirements capture.
- Definition of roles and responsibilities.
- Support task analysis to determine training needs.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Logistic support analysis.
- Information flow analysis.

****

**Implementation**

The Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view is represented using:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . It represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Process Flow diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . It represents Operational Activity flows.
- Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. An Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the operational process

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the operational process flow

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Service' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Condition' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implements' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurable Element' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity Edge' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Agent' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c9d805a-1c61-443c-a957-05f57eacb416"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">908843947</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48941402-a897-48e2-bf9b-5f0ad22e48e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">908843949</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c6c9c99-3f34-4749-991b-e2ac5aa07b7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">908843948</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view concerns capturing activity based behavior and flows. It describes the activities that are normally conducted in the course of achieving business goals that support a capability. It describes operational activities, their inputs/outputs, operational activity actions and flows between them.</p><p>The intended usage of the Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view includes:</p><ul><li>Description of activities and workflows.</li><li>Requirements capture.</li><li>Definition of roles and responsibilities.</li><li>Support task analysis to determine training needs.</li><li>Problem space definition.</li><li>Operational planning.</li><li>Logistic support analysis.</li><li>Information flow analysis.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Operational Processes (Op-Pr) view is represented using:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Processes diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Processes diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Process Flow diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Process Flow diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It represents Operational Activity flows.</li><li>Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities. An Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational Process" ac:alt="Operational Process"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the operational process</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational Process Flow" ac:alt="Operational Process Flow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_process_flow.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the operational process flow</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15c63dea-3f80-4b40-8fce-57688dcf587d"><ac:parameter ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Usage of an Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong> </p><p>Usage of an Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship.</p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span> </span></p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Extensions</strong></span></p><p><span class="fontstyle2">UML Property</span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927555 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00808: Operational Sequences

- page_id: `191927555`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927555/Operational+Sequences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:56:52.770+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

908539195

908539198

908539197

**Description**

The Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) express a time ordered examination of the operational exchanges as a result of a particular operational scenario. It provides a time-ordered analysis of the operational exchanges between participating nodes (Operational Performer roles) as a result of a particular operational scenario.

Operational Event/Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios, or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of actions in a scenario or critical sequence of events.

The intended usage of the Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) view includes:

- Analysis of operational events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional user requirements.
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

The Operational Sequences (Op-Sq) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- Operational Sequences (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. An Operational Sequences (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Sequences diagram

908539194

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Method' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

## PAGE 00809: Operational Signal

- page_id: `191929043`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929043/Operational+Signal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:38:04.250+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An Operational Signal is a specification of a kind of communication between operational performers in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Signal

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>An Operational Signal is a specification of a kind of communication between operational performers in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Signal</p>
````

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A state machine describing the behavior of a Operational Performer, depicting how the Operational Performer responds to various events and the actions.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML State Machine

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927560 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00811: Operational States

- page_id: `191927560`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927560/Operational+States
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:58.625+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

909091961

909091963

909091962

**Description**

The Operational States (Op-St) view captures the state-based behavior of an operational Operational Performer. The Operational States diagram models how and why an element changes in response to the environment. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

An Operational States view (Op-St) can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. It is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Process Flow diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. The Operational States (Op-St) view relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).

The intended usage of the Operational States (Op-St) view includes:

- Analysis of business events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

The Operational States (Op-St) view is represented by the Operational States diagram that is based on a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational States diagram

909091960

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Agent' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR2']

The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>An Operational States view (Op-St) can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. It is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Process Flow diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Process Flow diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Operational States (Op-St) view relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).</p><p>The intended usage of the Operational States (Op-St) view includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of business events.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Operational States (Op-St) view is represented by the Operational States diagram that is based on a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Operational States" ac:alt="Operational States"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_states.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational States" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Operational States diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccff8f14-0d61-4fc1-b122-abc47e62a012"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">909091960</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Agent" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational State Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Performer" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927562 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00812: Operational Structure

- page_id: `191927562`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927562/Operational+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:55:04.528+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

910945993

910945995

910945994

**Description**

An Operational Structure (Op-Sr) view identifies the operational exchange requirements between nodes. It defines operational architecture and exchange requirements necessary to support a specific set of Capabilities.

**Implementation**

An Operational Structure (Op-Sr) view is represented by the UML Class diagram.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927583 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00813: Operational Taxonomy

- page_id: `191927583`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927583/Operational+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:00:32.489+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911176512

911176514

911176513

**Description**

The Operational Taxonomy (Op-Tx) shows the main Operational Performers of the architectural scenario. It also illustrates the flows of information and materiel between these Operational Performers specified in the Operational Information model.

The **Operational **High Level**Taxonomy** diagram illustrates the primary scenario for which the architecture is intended.

**Implementation**

The Operational Taxonomy (Op-Tx) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . The primary purpose of the Operational Taxonomy diagram is to define capability requirements within an operational context. It may also be used to express a capability boundary. The Operational Taxonomy diagram can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. The intended usage of the Operational Taxonomy diagram includes:
  - Definition of operational concepts.
  - Elaboration of capability requirements.
  - Definition of collaboration needs.
  - Applying a local context to a capability.
  - Problem space definition.
  - Operational planning.
  - Supply chain analysis.
  - Allocation of activities to resources.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . It describes a mission, class of mission, or scenario and shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. With a help of a Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram you can describe the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The intended usage of the Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram includes:
  - 
    - Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.
    - Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.
    - Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.
- Operational Free Form Taxonomy diagram. This diagram can be used alternatively to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Taxonomy Table

911176511

1112822297

1112822299

1112822298

**Description**

The Operational Traceability describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the supporting operational activities and operational agents.

**Implementation**

The Operational Traceability is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. It shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational performers that those capabilities support.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. It shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926546 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00815: Operational viewpoint

- page_id: `191926546`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926546/Operational+viewpoint

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['operational-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306262672

306262674

306262673

“DoDAF-described Models in the Operational Viewpoint describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and resource flow exchanges required to conduct operations. A pure operational model is materiel independent. However, operations and their relationships may be influenced by new technologies, such as collaboration technology, where process improvements are in practice before policy can reflect the new procedures. There may be some cases, as well, in which it is necessary to document the way activities are performed, given the restrictions of current systems, to examine ways in which new systems could facilitate streamlining the activities. In such cases, operational models may have materiel constraints and requirements that need to be addressed. For this reason, it may be necessary to include some high-level system architectural data to augment information onto the operational models.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo **[*UPDM: Operational viewpoint*](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74).

Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.

306262671

**Views**

However, operations and their relationships may be influenced by new technologies, such as collaboration technology, where process improvements are in practice before policy can reflect the new procedures. There may be some cases, as well, in which it is necessary to document the way activities are performed, given the restrictions of current systems, to examine ways in which new systems could facilitate streamlining the activities. In such cases, operational models may have materiel constraints and requirements that need to be addressed. For this reason, it may be necessary to include some high-level system architectural data to augment information onto the operational models.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p><p>The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo <em><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)"> </span></em><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74"><em><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)">UPDM: Operational viewpoint</span></em><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)"> </span></a><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)">.</span><span class="watch-title" title="UPDM: Strategic (part 1 of 2)"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d542d52-a4eb-482e-aad4-bc504a788e27"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3cbb6a5a-7525-444d-a8c8-905c04da1561"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306262671</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="96cb6c1a-2a36-41c1-b537-560cfa60e460" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928887 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00816: OperationalBToSSync 1

- page_id: `191928887`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928887/OperationalBToSSync+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-26T07:54:37.561+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

OperationalBToSSync[1]

**Description**

No compatible Operational Interface. Thisvalidation rule checks whether Operational Ports and Operational Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Operational Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Create Operational Port - creates a new Operational Port typed by a compatible Operational Interface.
- Choose Compatible Operational Interface - changes an existing Operational Port type to a compatible Operational Interface.
- Add Missing Flow Property - adds a new Flow Property to an Operational Interface that types an Operational Port.
- Reverse Direction of Operational Port - changes the Operational Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of an Operational Interface that types an Operational Port.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The Operational Port *iMedical care request* is created using Behavior to Structure Synchronization solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">validation rule checks whether Operational Ports and Operational Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Operational Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d88639d5-40f1-443d-8985-e8e9b51ab799">Diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Create Operational Port </strong>- creates a new Operational Port typed by a compatible Operational Interface.</li><li><strong>Choose Compatible Operational Interface </strong>- changes an existing Operational Port type to a compatible Operational Interface.</li><li><strong>Add Missing Flow Property </strong>- adds a new Flow Property to an Operational Interface that types an Operational Port.</li><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Reverse Direction of Operational Port </strong>- changes the Operational Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of an Operational Interface that types an Operational Port.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_behavior_to_structure_operational_ports_and_interfaces_validation_rule_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="OperationalBToSSync 1" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Operational Port <em>iMedical care request</em> is created using Behavior to Structure Synchronization solver.</h6>

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

## PAGE 00818: OperationalBToSSync 3

- page_id: `191928891`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928891/OperationalBToSSync+3
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-08T14:00:21.104+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

OperationalBToSSync[3]

**Description**

No compatible Operational Exchange realization. The third validation rule checks realized Operational Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Operational Exchanges are realized on Operational Connectors that connect Operational Ports typed by compatible Operational Interface.Themissing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Realize Missing Operational Exchange - realizes existing compatible Operational Exchange found in the behavior model on the Operational Connector.
- Open Operational Exchange Manager - opens the Operational Exchange Manager dialog.
- Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking - opens the Project Options dialog to turn on/off the Exchange realization checking.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realizing a missing Operational Exchange on the Connector in the Operational Internal Connectivity Diagram.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926548 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00823: OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

- page_id: `191926548`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926548/OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:16:59.714+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-1-high-level-operational-concept-graphic', 'ov-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306325535

306325538

306325536

**Description**

The OV-1 describes a mission, class of mission, or scenario. It shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. It describes the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The OV-1 is the pictorial representation of the written content of the AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. Graphics alone are not sufficient for capturing the necessary architectural data.

The OV-1 provides a graphical depiction of what the architecture is about and an idea of the players and operations involved. An OV-1 can be used to orient and focus detailed discussions. Its main use is to aid human communication, and it is intended for presentation to high-level decision-makers.

The intended usage of the OV-1 includes:

- Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.
- Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.
- Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.

**Implementation**

OV-1 can be represented using:

- OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_1.png']
- OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- Link to an external document.
- UML Composite Structure Diagram.

306325534

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='High Level Operational Concept' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

It shows the main operational concepts and interesting or unique aspects of operations. It describes the interactions between the subject architecture and its environment, and between the architecture and external systems. The OV-1 is the pictorial representation of the written content of the AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. Graphics alone are not sufficient for capturing the necessary architectural data.</p><p>The OV-1 provides a graphical depiction of what the architecture is about and an idea of the players and operations involved. An OV-1 can be used to orient and focus detailed discussions. Its main use is to aid human communication, and it is intended for presentation to high-level decision-makers.</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Putting an operational situation or scenario into context.</li><li>Providing a tool for discussion and presentation; for example, aids industry engagement in acquisition.</li><li>Providing an aggregate illustration of the details within the published high-level organization of more detailed information in published architectures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" ac:alt="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li><p>Link to an external document.</p></li><li><p>UML Composite Structure Diagram.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5451aa2-f71c-462b-809f-c02b93cefd42"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306325534</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="High Level Operational Concept" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Concept Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926551 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00824: OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `191926551`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926551/OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:17:29.943+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-2-operational-resource-flow-description', 'ov-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306477774

306477776

306477775

**Description**

The OV-2 DoDAF-described Model applies the context of the operational capability to a community of anticipated users. The primary purpose of the OV-2 is to define capability requirements within an operational context. The OV-2 may also be used to express a capability boundary.

New to DoDAF V2.0, the OV-2 can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. A specific application of the OV-2 is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) flows. The logical pattern need not correspond to specific organizations, systems or locations, allowing Resource Flows to be established without prescribing the way that the Resource Flows are handled and without prescribing solutions.

The intended usage of the OV-2 includes:

- Definition of operational concepts.
- Elaboration of capability requirements.
- Definition of collaboration needs.
- Applying a local context to a capability.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.
- Allocation of activities to resources.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

TheOperational Connectorestablished in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model.

In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or Swimlanes on the activities.

OV-2 diagram name in DoDAF differs from the one in MODAF, though the diagram concept in both architecture frameworks is the same. OV-2 is one of the starting views of the architecture for DoDAF and requires the Strategic Viewpoint to be developed for MODAF.

306477773

**Related elements**

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23aa173c-e196-4a06-85dd-349d918f0e30"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306477774</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d0cb756-28a3-45c5-93f0-d8b379147eef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306477776</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78ca2f18-683d-42c1-a5b7-1fe0233b0489"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306477775</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The OV-2 DoDAF-described Model applies the context of the operational capability to a community of anticipated users. The primary purpose of the OV-2 is to define capability requirements within an operational context. The OV-2 may also be used to express a capability boundary.</p><p>New to DoDAF V2.0, the OV-2 can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. A specific application of the OV-2 is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) flows. The logical pattern need not correspond to specific organizations, systems or locations, allowing Resource Flows to be established without prescribing the way that the Resource Flows are handled and without prescribing solutions.</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-2 includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of operational concepts.</li><li>Elaboration of capability requirements.</li><li>Definition of collaboration needs.</li><li>Applying a local context to a capability.</li><li>Problem space definition.</li><li>Operational planning.</li><li>Supply chain analysis.</li><li>Allocation of activities to resources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-2 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ac:alt="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Operational Connector </span>established in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> model or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> model.</p><p>In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or Swimlanes on the activities.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">OV-2 diagram name in DoDAF differs from the one in MODAF, though the diagram concept in both architecture frameworks is the same. OV-2 is one of the starting views of the architecture for DoDAF and requires the Strategic Viewpoint to be developed for MODAF.</span> <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926555 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00825: OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description

- page_id: `191926555`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926555/OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Internal+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:17:52.609+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-2-operational-resource-flow-internal-description', 'ov-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306683033

306683035

306683034

**Description**

A specific application of the Operational Resource Flow Internal Description is to describe a logical pattern of resource (information, funding, personnel, or materiel) internal flows.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Operational Connector established in an OV-2 can be realized by resources and their interactions in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model.

In developing an Architectural Description, OV-2 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] are often the starting points and these may be developed iteratively. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Resource Flows, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5b, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Resource Flows, which can be shown as annotations or Swimlanes on the activities.

306683032

## PAGE 00826: OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `191926559`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926559/OV-3+Operational+Resource+Flow+Matrix

- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:18:30.663+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-3-operational-resource-flow-matrix', 'ov-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

312389490

312389492

312389491

**Description**

The OV-3 addresses operational Resource Flows exchanged between Operational Activities and locations.

Resource Flows provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on Resource Flows that cross the capability boundary.

The intended usage of the OV-3 includes definition of interoperability requirements.

**Implementation**

OV-3 can be represented using:

- OV-3 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_3.png']
- OV-3 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Performers). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- OV-3 spreadsheet report.

312389489

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926564 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00827: OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

- page_id: `191926564`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926564/OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:18:56.222+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-4-organizational-relationships-chart', 'ov-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

312601796

312601798

312601797

**Description**

The OV-4 shows organizational structures and interactions. The organizations shown may be civil or military. The OV-4 exists in two forms; role-based (e.g., a typical brigade command structure) and actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency).

A role-based OV-4 shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. There are no prescribed resource interactions in DoDAF V2.0: the architect should select an appropriate interaction type from the DM2 or add a new one. Interactions illustrate the fundamental roles and management responsibilities, such as supervisory reporting, Command and Control (C2) relationships, collaboration and so on.

An actual OV-4 shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time, and is used to provide context to other parts of the architecture such as AV-1 and the CVs.

The intended usage of the role-based OV-4 includes:

- Organizational analysis.
- Definition of human roles.
- Operational analysis.

The intended usage of the actual OV-4 includes:

- Identify architecture stakeholders.
- Identify process owners.
- Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

OV-4 can be represented using:

- OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The organizations that are modeled using OV-4 may also appear in other models, for example in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] as organizational constituents of a capability or a resource and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] where organizations own projects. In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], for instance, the organizational resources defined in a typical OV-4 may be part of a capability or resources. Also, actual organizations may form elements of a fielded capability which realizes the requirements at the system-level (again, this may be depicted on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']).

312601794

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Person' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Command' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Competence' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Post' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Person' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fills Post' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Responsible For' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Responsibility' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Provides Competence' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
