### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb5fa21c-96bb-4870-bd5b-97d48c6bde8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">312601796</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afe4f21d-6d36-411e-be6c-592356e4a7d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">312601798</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16cf1c27-75f9-44fd-8ddd-3946d9638109"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">312601797</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The OV-4 shows organizational structures and interactions. The organizations shown may be civil or military. The OV-4 exists in two forms; role-based (e.g., a typical brigade command structure) and actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency).</p><p>A role-based OV-4 shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. There are no prescribed resource interactions in DoDAF V2.0: the architect should select an appropriate interaction type from the DM2 or add a new one. Interactions illustrate the fundamental roles and management responsibilities, such as supervisory reporting, Command and Control (C2) relationships, collaboration and so on.</p><p>An actual OV-4 shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time, and is used to provide context to other parts of the architecture such as AV-1 and the CVs.</p><p>The intended usage of the role-based OV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Organizational analysis.</li><li>Definition of human roles.</li><li>Operational analysis.</li></ul><p>The intended usage of the actual OV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Identify architecture stakeholders.</li><li>Identify process owners.</li><li>Illustrate current or future organization structures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ac:alt="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The organizations that are modeled using OV-4 may also appear in other models, for example in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> as organizational constituents of a capability or a resource and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> where organizations own projects. In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>, for instance, the organizational resources defined in a typical OV-4 may be part of a capability or resources. Also, actual organizations may form elements of a fielded capability which realizes the requirements at the system-level (again, this may be depicted on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="928e7737-dfc4-4ed5-baab-42c1410f36e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">312601794</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Organization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Post" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Person" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Command" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Competence" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Responsibility" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Requires Competence" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Organization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Post" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Person" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Fills Post" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Responsible For" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Responsibility" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Provides Competence" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-4 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instantiating structures" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926569 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00828: OV-5 Operational Activity Model

- page_id: `191926569`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926569/OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-24T14:12:12.617+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-5', 'ov-5a-operational-activity-decomposition-tree', 'ov-5b-operational-activity-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

312777547

312777549

312777548

**Description**

The OV-5a and the OV-5b describe the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks); Input/Output flows between activities, and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architectural Description.

The OV-5a and OV-5b describes the operational activities that are being conducted within the mission or scenario.

The OV-5a and OV-5b can be used to:

- Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy.
- Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities.
- Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinized further.
- Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6b Operational State Transition Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] , and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

The OV-5b describes the operational, business, and defense portion of the intelligence community activities associated with the Architectural Description, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the activities.
- Resources exchanged between activities.
- External interchanges (from/to business activities that are outside the scope of the model).

An Operational Activity is what work is required, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical activities and locations in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] are used to represent the structure which carries out the Operational Activities. Operational Activities are realized as System Functions (described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']) or Service Functions (described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']) which are the how to the Operational Activities what, i.e., they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The intended usage of the OV-5a and OV-5b includes:

- Description of activities and workflows.
- Requirements capture.
- Definition of roles and responsibilities.
- Support task analysis to determine training needs.
- Problem space definition.
- Operational planning.
- Logistic support analysis.
- Information flow analysis.

**Implementation**

OV-5 can be represented using:

- OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_5a.png']
- OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_5b.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

You can also create OV-5 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin.

The OV-5 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model are, to a degree, complements of each other. The OV-5 focuses on the operational activities whereas [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model focuses on the operational activities in relation to locations. Due to the relationship between locations and operational activities, these types of models should normally be developed together.

312777546

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926575 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00829: OV-5 Operational Activity Model BPD

- page_id: `191926575`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926575/OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T07:25:30.982+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>OV-5 Operational Activity Model (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926805 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00830: OV-6a Operational Node Parametric

- page_id: `191926805`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926805/OV-6a+Operational+Node+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:54.256+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497663498

497663500

497663499

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497663497

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94215050-1653-48a0-833c-a69214be827f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497663498</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d3d689d-4e35-47ef-97eb-55fa116e4b8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497663500</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8405aa85-f232-4ec5-83ed-eaad0d15c5d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497663499</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint block]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c16fea62-76ff-4d4d-ae92-0dd3ceae57cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497663497</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00831: OV-6a Operational Resource Parametric

- page_id: `191926579`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926579/OV-6a+Operational+Resource+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:52.631+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497172585

497172588

497172586

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497172583

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e3ee17-8ddb-4448-9ae7-cea2cdcbf86d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497172583</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926576 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00832: OV-6a Operational Rules Model

- page_id: `191926576`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926576/OV-6a+Operational+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-19T07:56:47.533+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6a-operational-rules-model', 'ov-6a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393268360

393268362

393268361

**Description**

An OV-6a specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the enterprise. At a top-level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic and provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed rules and behavioral definitions that should occur later in the Architectural definition process.

The intended usage of the OV-6a includes:

- Definition of doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Definition of business rules.
- Identification of operational constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6a can be represented using:

- OV-6a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='OV-6a.PNG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Resource Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- OV-6a spreadsheet report.

Rules defined in an OV-6a may optionally be presented in any other OV. For example, a rule “battle damage assessment shall be carried out under fair weather conditions” may be linked to the Conduct BDA activity in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. Any natural language rule presented (e.g., in a diagram note) should also be listed in OV-6a.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

393268359

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Constraint' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926580 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00833: OV-6b Operational State Transition Description

- page_id: `191926580`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926580/OV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:52.689+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6b-operational-state-transition-description', 'ov-6b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393571094

393571096

393571095

**Description**

The OV-6b is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).

The intended usage of the OV-6b includes:

- Analysis of business events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

OV-6b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OV-6b reflects the fact that the explicit sequencing of activities in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

393571093

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The OV-6b relates events and states. A change of state is called a transition. Actions may be associated with a given state or with the transition between states in response to stimuli (e.g., triggers and events).</p><p>The intended usage of the OV-6b UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="OV-6b Operational State Transition Description" ac:alt="OV-6b Operational State Transition Description"><ri:attachment <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926582 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00834: OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `191926582`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926582/OV-6c+Operational+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T07:35:32.625+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: ['ov-6c-operational-event-trace-description', 'ov-6c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393648787

393648789

393648788

**Description**

The OV-6c provides a time-ordered examination of the Resource Flows as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event/Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios, or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of actions in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The OV-6c can be used by itself or in conjunction with an OV-6b State Transition Description to describe the dynamic behavior of activities.

The intended usage of the OV-6c includes:

- Analysis of operational events.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional user requirements.
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

OV-6c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The information content of messages in an OV-6c may be related with the Resource Flows in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and information entities in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

393648786

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

An OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>An OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927267 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00839: P1 Resource Types

- page_id: `191927267`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927267/P1+Resource+Types
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.015+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p1-resource-types', 'p1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

825460242

825460244

825460243

**Description**

The P1 Resource Types view specifies the types of Resources, Technology and Competences required for the architecture. It can also be used to specify properties of Resources. This view collects together all the Resource Types in the architecture. It can also be used to identify Technologies and Competences and map these to resource types. Technologies and Competences can also be set against a timeline indicating when they are expected to be in use.

The P1 view may be used for:

- Identifying Resource Taxonomies.
- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR trends analysis.
- Recruitment planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.
- Definition of performance characteristics.
- Identification of non-functional requirements (input to SRD).

**Implementation**

The P1 view can be represented using:

- P1 Resource Types diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Systems to Systems Matrix. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P1_typical.png']
- P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Resource Types Technology Forecast table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- P1 Resource Types Service Provision which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The ServiceInterfaces are used as the row elements and the Resources are used as the column elements. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

825460240

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927303 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00840: P2 Resource Internal Structure

- page_id: `191927303`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927303/P2+Resource+Internal+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:45:02.872+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p2-resource-structure', 'p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

827013152

827013155

827013153

**Description**

The P2i Resource Internal Structure view addresses the composition and (high-level) interaction of resources.

The P2i view links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. The P2i view may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view(i.e. in a to-be architecture) and so there may be many alternative Resource view suites that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2']view may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the P2i to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders.

The P2i view can be used to specify typical (or template) organization structures, and also identify how human resources interact with each other and with systems.

This view can provide a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The P2i view depicts interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect.

Resources may be decomposed in the P2i view to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition that the architect sees fit. This view may also identify the physical asset (e.g. platforms) at which resources are deployed and can show the operational nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view may well be the logical representation of the resource shown in the P2i view.

The P2i view may be used for:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- Human – System interactions.
- Typical Organization structures.
- Interface requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability configuration definition).

**Implementation**

The P2i view can be represented using:

- P2 Resource Internal Structure diagram, which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P2.png']
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

827013151

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Connector' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Port' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The P2i view may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L2 Logical Scenario" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view </span>(i.e. in a to-be architecture) and so there may be many alternative Resource view suites that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L2 Logical Internal Scenario" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2 ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>view may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the P2i to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders.</p><p>The P2i view can be used to specify typical (or template) organization structures, and also identify how human resources interact with each other and with systems.</p><p>This view can provide a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The P2i view depicts interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect. </p><p>Resources may be decomposed in the P2i view to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition that the architect sees fit. This view may also identify the physical asset (e.g. platforms) at which resources are deployed and can show the operational nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L2 Logical Scenario" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view may well be the logical representation of the resource shown in the P2i view.</p><p>The P2i view may be used for:</p><ul><li>Definition of system concepts.</li><li>Definition of system options.</li><li>Human – System interactions.</li><li>Typical Organization structures.</li><li>Interface requirements capture.</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>System integration management.</li><li>Operational planning (capability configuration definition).</li></ul><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3693a30d-5523-4d04-8877-cd1f953b5116">Implementation</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p>The P2i view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">P2 Resource Internal Structure diagram, which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.<br /></span><ac:image ac:title="P2 Resource Structure diagram" ac:alt="P2 Resource Structure diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="P2 Resource Internal Structure" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="73681dc0-918c-48bc-ace7-e00bd668534e">UML Composite Structure diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br style="color: rgb(255,0,0);" /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="73681dc0-918c-48bc-ace7-e00bd668534e">SysML Internal Block diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa68539f-9ac3-43f8-9eb1-7554aff5d590"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">827013151</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Interface" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Port" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Connector" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Port" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Information" ac:macro-id="57e3183c-2a55-4fb0-bfd2-a50db7644500" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P2 Viewpoint is concerned with the composition and (high-level) interaction of resources.

**Implementation**

The P2 view canberepresented using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927309 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00842: P3 Resource Connectivity

- page_id: `191927309`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927309/P3+Resource+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:45:25.232+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p3-resource-connectivity']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

828276772

828276775

828276774

**Description**

The P3 Resource Connectivity view describes communications networks and pathways that link communications systems, and provides details regarding their configuration. The purpose of P3 view is to provide a comprehensive specification of how systems are connected, what interfaces each system exposes (ports), the hardware interface used and the protocols transmitted across the interface.

The networks and pathways documented through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L3 Node Interactions' space='UAF12P2024xR2']view.

The P3 view focuses on the physical characteristics of each link by specifying attributes. An example of this might be the geographic location and layout of network components such as routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters.

**Implementation**

The P3 view can be represented using:

- P3 Resource Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3.PNG']
- P3 Resource Role Connectivity table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P3_role_connectivity.png']
- P3 spreadsheet report.

828276771

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927313 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00843: P4 Resource Functions

- page_id: `191927313`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927313/P4+Resource+Functions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.178+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p4-resource-functions', 'p4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

828581287

828581290

828581288

**Description**

The P4 Resource Functions view addresses resource (human and non-human) functions – i.e. the activities performed by Resources.

The primary purposes of the P4 view are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e. that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.
- Provide implementation-specific realizations of the operational activities specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of data between functions.

The P4 view is the systems view counterpart to the Activity Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024xR2']) of the operational view.

The P4 view may be used for:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

The P4 view can be represented using:

- P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- P4 Resource Functions diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='P4_matrix.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

828581285

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927321 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00844: P5 Resource States

- page_id: `191927321`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927321/P5+Resource+States
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.320+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p5-resource-states', 'p5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

828879834

828879836

828879835

**Description**

The P5 Resource States view describes how Resources change state in response to events and other stimuli. The P5 view identifies the states a Resource can be, the allowable changes between those states, and the triggers that cause the state changes.

The P5 view may be used to:

- Define states, events and state transitions (behavioural modelling).
- Identify constraints (input to System Requirements Document).

**Implementation**

The P5 view can be represented using a P5 Resource States diagram which is based on the UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

828879833

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource State Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927323 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00845: P6 Resource Sequence

- page_id: `191927323`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927323/P6+Resource+Sequence
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.351+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p6-resource-sequence', 'p6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

829003730

829003732

829003731

**Description**

The P6 Resource Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between Resources. These are usually depicted as event-trace diagrams and will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

P6 products are valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

The P6 view may be used for:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements (input to System Requirement Document).

**Implementation**

The P6 view can be represented using a P6 Resource Sequence diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

829003729

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927326 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00846: P7 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `191927326`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927326/P7+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.394+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p7-physical-data-model', 'p7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

829238245

829238248

829238246

**Description**

The P7 Physical Data Model view defines the structure of data used by the Resources in the architecture. It is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L7 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2']L7 Logical Data Model is implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (eg entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

The P7 view can be represented using:

- P7 Physical Data Model diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='P7.png']

- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

829238243

**Related elements**

- Property
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It is used to describe how the information represented in the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L7 Logical Data Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">L7 Logical Data Mode</span>l</ac:link-body></ac:link> is implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (eg entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The P7 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>P7 Physical Data Model diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title=" P7 Physical Data Model diagram" ac:alt=" P7 Physical Data Model diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="P7 Physical Data Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cf0a0a08-39fd-44b2-bdf9-aa0cb5e98722">UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cf0a0a08-39fd-44b2-bdf9-aa0cb5e98722">SysML Block Definition diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="218d97ba-97c2-4714-9473-16344ee6a907"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">829238243</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li>Property</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Information Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e5486927-a967-4ef6-8626-4c72c2a9d5c3" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927329 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00847: P8 Resource Constraints

- page_id: `191927329`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927329/P8+Resource+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:24:24.410+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p8-resource-constraints', 'p8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

830788941

830788943

830788942

**Description**

The P8 Resource Constraints view specifies functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the Resource viewpoint). It constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Resource architecture. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

The P8 view may be used to:

- Define implementation logic.
- Identify resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The P8 view can be represented using:

- P8 Resource Constraints table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P8.PNG']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P8 Resource Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.
- P8 spreadsheet report.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

830788940

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Constraint' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

## PAGE 00848: P8 Resource Parametric

- page_id: `191927332`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927332/P8+Resource+Parametric

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P8 Resource Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

498407802

498407804

498407803

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to constrain the properties of another Capability Configuration. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

498407801

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c9e7bad-dde2-4e6f-8d27-bdcf17c67107"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498407801</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00849: Parameters Pm

- page_id: `191928091`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928091/Parameters+Pm
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:15:03.364+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1339611046

1339611068

1339611058

**Definition**

Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.

**Implementation**

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Environment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Environment**diagram . It shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.
- The**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Risks diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**diagram. It shows the relevant risks along with associated measures like the likelihood of occurrence and potential negative consequences.

The Risk and Environment diagrams can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters).

1339611045

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929052 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00850: Performs In Context

- page_id: `191929052`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929052/Performs+In+Context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:56:57.615+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstraction relationship that relates an Operational Action to a Operational Role, or a Function Action to a Resource Role. It indicates that the action can be carried out by the role when used in a specific context or configuration.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An abstraction relationship that relates an Operational Action to a Operational Role, or a Function Action to a Resource Role. It indicates that the action can be carried out by the role when used in a specific context or configuration.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Abstraction</p><p><br /></p>
````

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A type of a human being used to define the characteristics that need to be described for Actual Persons (e.g. properties such as address, telephone number, nationality, etc).

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00852: Personnel

- page_id: `191927709`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927709/Personnel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:46:39.183+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912116444

912116446

912116445

The Personnel (Pr) domain describes the human factors. It aims to clarify the role of Human Factors (HF) when creating architectures in order to facilitate both Human Factors Integration (HFI) and systems engineering (SE).

912116442

**Views**

It aims to clarify the role of Human Factors (HF) when creating architectures in order to facilitate both Human Factors Integration (HFI) and systems engineering (SE).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e99e7f77-7b4c-4894-a08c-ba44ed1d58a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">912116442</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="01a09215-5fee-440b-9268-77054d289390" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927710 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00853: Personnel Connectivity

- page_id: `191927710`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927710/Personnel+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:00:03.283+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912198739

912198741

912198740

**Description**

The Personnel Connectivity (Pr-Cn) table references the resource structure, connectors and interfaces in a specific context. It defines the physical resources, e.g. capability configuration(s)/system(s) and interactions necessary to implement a specific set of Operational Performer(s). Can be used to represent communications networks and pathways that link communications resources and provides details regarding their configuration.

The Personnel Connectivity Internal diagram captures the possible interactions between organizational resources, including command and control relationships. Interactions typically illustrate the fundamental roles and management responsibilities.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Connectivity (Pr-Cn) domain is implemented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram. It addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Role-based Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- Reports (.xlsx): - Personnel Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet. - Personnel Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### An example of the Personnel Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Connectivity table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927720 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00854: Personnel Constraints

- page_id: `191927720`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927720/Personnel+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:05:36.828+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

48144373

48144375

48144374

**Description**

The Personnel Constraints (Pr-Ct) domain defines limitations, constraints, and performance parameters for resources, their interactions, performed functions, and data. It specifies traditional textual rules/non-functional requirements. The addition of SysML parametrics provide a computational means of defining resource constraints within a specific context.

The intended usage of the Personnel Constraints (Pr-Ct) view includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Constraints (Pr-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- Personnel Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Constraints table

48144372

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

It specifies traditional textual rules/non-functional requirements. The addition of SysML parametrics provide a computational means of defining resource constraints within a specific context.</p><p>The intended usage of the Personnel Constraints (Pr-Ct) view includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of implementation logic.</li><li>Identification of resource constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Personnel Constraints (Pr-Ct) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Constraints table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Constraints Definition diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Constraints Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Personnel Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel_constraints_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Personnel Constraints" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927790 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00855: Personnel Motivation

- page_id: `191927790`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927790/Personnel+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-28T16:59:56.954+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Personnel Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Personnel Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

**Description**

The Personnel Parameters (Pe-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Personnel Parameters (Pe-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Parameters (Pe-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Environment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927727 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00857: Personnel Processes

- page_id: `191927727`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927727/Personnel+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:49:26.621+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1364352172

1364352174

1364352173

**Description**

The Personnel Processes (Pr-Pr) domain describes the functions that are normally conducted in the course of implementing operational activities in support of capabilities. It describes the functions, their inputs/outputs, function actions and flows between them.

The Personnel Processes (Pr-Pr) domain addresses human and system functionality.

The primary purposes of The Personnel Processes (Pr-Pr) domain are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The intended usage of the The Personnel Processes (Pr-Pr) domain includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Processes (Pr-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Processes Flow diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- Personnel Processes Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Processes diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Process Flow diagram

## PAGE 00858: Personnel Roadmap

- page_id: `191927737`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927737/Personnel+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:49:09.240+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912371485

912371487

912371486

**Description**

The Personnel Roadmap (Pr-Rm) domain defines the staffing and training of resources. It defines the requirements and functions to ensure that actual persons with the right competencies, and in the right numbers, are available to fulfill actual posts.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Roadmap (Pr-Rm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Forecast diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Evolution diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Forecast table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927752 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00859: Personnel Sequences

- page_id: `191927752`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927752/Personnel+Sequences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:58:57.785+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1278268829

1278268831

1278268830

**Description**

The Personnel Sequences (Ps-Sq) domain expresses the time-ordered examination of the interactions between operational resources (roles). Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The Personnel Sequences (Ps-Sq) domain is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating operational resource or resource role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of The Personnel Sequences (Ps-Sq) view includes:

- Analysis of operational resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Sequences (Ps-Sq) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] that is based on interaction.
- Personnel Sequences (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Sequences diagram

1278268827

## PAGE 00860: Personnel States

- page_id: `191927758`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927758/Personnel+States

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912538451

912538454

912538452

**Description**

The Personnel States (Pr-St) domain captures state-based behavior of an organizational resource. It is a graphical representation of states of an organizational resource and how that organizational resource responds to various events and actions.

The intended usage of the Personnel States (Pr-St) domain includes:

- Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavior modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

The Personnel States (Pr-St) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR2'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel States diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927760 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00861: Personnel Structure

- page_id: `191927760`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927760/Personnel+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:50:10.372+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912616671

912616673

912616672

**Description**

The Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain shows the analysis, e.g. evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations as it provides a means to capture different solution architectures. The detailed analysis (trade-off, what-if etc.) is carried out using the Resource Constraints view. It illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations required to meet an operational need.

The Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain is divided in two forms:

- Typical (e.g., a typical brigade command structure). It shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. The intended usage of the typical form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:
  - Organizational analysis.
  - Definition of human roles.
  - Operational analysis.
- Actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency). It shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time. The intended usage of the actual form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:
  - Identify architecture stakeholders.
  - Identify process owners.
  - Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Structure diagram

The detailed analysis (trade-off, what-if etc.) is carried out using the Resource Constraints view. It illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations required to meet an operational need.</p><p>The Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain is divided in two forms:</p><ul><li>Typical (e.g., a typical brigade command structure). It shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources.<p>The intended usage of the typical form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Organizational analysis.</li><li>Definition of human roles.</li><li>Operational analysis.</li></ul></li><li>Actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency). It shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time.<br /><p>The intended usage of the actual form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Identify architecture stakeholders.</li><li>Identify process owners.</li><li>Illustrate current or future organization structures.</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain is represented by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Structure diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Personnel Structure" ac:alt="Personnel Structure"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel_structure.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Personnel Structure" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Personnel Structure <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927777 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00862: Personnel Taxonomy

- page_id: `191927777`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927777/Personnel+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:00:11.376+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912730168

912730170

912730169

**Description**

The Personnel Taxonomy (Pr-Tx) domain shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources.

The Personnel Taxonomy (Pr-Tx) domain is divided in two forms:

- Typical (e.g., a typical brigade command structure). It shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. The intended usage of the typical form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:
  - Organizational analysis.
  - Definition of human roles.
  - Operational analysis.
- Actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency). It shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time. The intended usage of the actual form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:
  - Identify architecture stakeholders.
  - Identify process owners.
  - Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

The Personnel Taxonomy (Pr-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Personnel Taxonomy diagram

912730166

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Physical Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Responsibility' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources.<p>The intended usage of the typical form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Organizational analysis.</li><li>Definition of human roles.</li><li>Operational analysis.</li></ul></li><li>Actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency). It shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time.<br /><p>The intended usage of the actual form of the Personnel Structure (Pr-Sr) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Identify architecture stakeholders.</li><li>Identify process owners.</li><li>Illustrate current or future organization structures.</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Personnel Taxonomy (Pr-Tx) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Taxonomy table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Taxonomy table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. ## PAGE 00863: Personnel Traceability

- page_id: `191927789`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927789/Personnel+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-02-23T13:17:29.193+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

19717967

19717988

19717978

**Description**

The Personnel Traceability (Ps-Tr) view focuses on the traceability between operational activities and functions that implement them. It depicts the mapping of functions (performed by organizational resources) to operational activities, and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful function performed by an organizational resource or solution.

**Implementation**

Personnel Traceability view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

19717966

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
