<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927266 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00866: Physical Resource viewpoint

- page_id: `191927266`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927266/Physical+Resource+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:51:12.350+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0
- labels: ['physical-resource-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

825373278

825373281

825373280

The Physical Resource viewpoint is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. This viewpoint associates systems resources to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and/or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service viewpoint' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service viewpoint' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

825373277

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19fd6c29-f7e3-4968-979a-82fd473d851f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">825373278</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af95d112-572f-4ebb-9c7a-3cf473329aec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">825373281</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47cc4bd4-d05e-4b14-8a5c-b4fa61459ee2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">825373280</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The</span> Physical Resource viewpoint is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span>This viewpoint associates systems resources to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logical viewpoint" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> and/or the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service viewpoint" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service viewpoint" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logical viewpoint" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="570d664e-464d-4087-8e45-82ee5fae9956"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">825373277</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5d7c5fb7-e17d-453c-aa8b-8c10afcbb36b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929262 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00867: Ports migration

- page_id: `191929262`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929262/Ports+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-06-08T14:59:21.018+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The approach for working with ports changed after the migration from UPDM2, as outlined below:

1. For all UPDM2 ports (Node Port and Resource Port), new Operational / Resource Interfaces are created and set as the port types. Separate interfaces are created for each of the ports. The name will be the same as the Type of the port with the keyword In, Out, or Inout added at the end according to the Flow Properties summarized in #2 below (for example, Data In). The location of the keywords will be next to the owner of the port. The new Operational / Resource Interface will be applied as a Type for port. No interface will be created if a port has no type.
2. Flow Properties are created for all the Interfaces (Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces). The Flow Property type is a Conveyed Item that is conveyed by the connector. The Flow Property direction is specified according to the information flow or relation direction from the current port perspective. If there is no Conveyed Item on the connector between ports, the Flow Property is created, the Port Type from UPDM2 is set as a type, and the direction is inout. If the Types of Ports are the same, the same interface can be reused. The new Flow Property will not be created if the type was Service Interface in UPDM2.
3. If Service / Request Ports interfaces are already created, they also have operations. Flow Properties will be created according to the parameters of these operations. The same number of Flow Properties and operations will have parameters with the same types. The Flow property direction will be the same as the parameter direction.
4. Services Access is migrated to Capability Configuration for Request/Service Ports (the new interface, Operational/Resource interface, will be created with a Flow Property).

The validation rules are updated according to all changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The approach for working with ports changed after the migration from UPDM2, as outlined below:</p><ol><li>For all UPDM2 ports (Node Port and Resource Port), new Operational / Resource Interfaces are created and set as the port types. Separate interfaces are created for each of the ports. The name will be the same as the Type of the port with the keyword In, Out, or Inout added at the end according to the Flow Properties summarized in #2 below (for example, Data In). The location of the keywords will be next to the owner of the port. The new Operational / Resource Interface will be applied as a Type for port. No interface will be created if a port has no type.</li><li>Flow Properties are created for all the Interfaces (Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces). The Flow Property type is a Conveyed Item that is conveyed by the connector. The Flow Property direction is specified according to the information flow or relation direction from the current port perspective. If there is no Conveyed Item on the connector between ports, the Flow Property is created, the Port Type from UPDM2 is set as a type, and the direction is inout. If the Types of Ports are the same, the same interface can be reused. The new Flow Property will not be created if the type was Service Interface in UPDM2.</li><li>If Service / Request Ports interfaces are already created, they also have operations. Flow Properties will be created according to the parameters of these operations. The same number of Flow Properties and operations will have parameters with the same types. The Flow property direction will be the same as the parameter direction.</li><li>Services Access is migrated to Capability Configuration for Request/Service Ports (the new interface, Operational/Resource interface, will be created with a Flow Property).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68272549-bbab-4fbb-abfe-4c68ad685748"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The validation rules are updated according to all changes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929242 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00868: Ports migration in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `191929242`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929242/Ports+migration+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T11:07:07.246+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The approach for working with ports changed after the migration from UPDM2, as outlined below:

1. For all UPDM2 ports (Node Port and Resource Port), new Operational / Resource Interfaces are created and set as the port types. Separate interfaces are created for each of the ports. The name will be the same as the Type of the port with the keyword In, Out, or Inout added at the end according to the Flow Properties summarized in #2 below (for example, Data In). The location of the keywords will be next to the owner of the port. The new Operational / Resource Interface will be applied as a Type for port. No interface will be created if a port has no type.
2. Flow Properties are created for all the Interfaces (Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces). The Flow Property type is a Conveyed Item that is conveyed by the connector. The Flow Property direction is specified according to the information flow or relation direction from the current port perspective. If there is no Conveyed Item on the connector between ports, the Flow Property is created, the Port Type from UPDM2 is set as a type, and the direction is inout. If the Types of Ports are the same, the same interface can be reused. The new Flow Property will not be created if the type was Service Interface in UPDM2.
3. If Service / Request Ports interfaces are already created, they also have operations. Flow Properties will be created according to the parameters of these operations. The same number of Flow Properties and operations will have parameters with the same types. The Flow property direction will be the same as the parameter direction.
4. Services Access is migrated to Resource Service for Request/Service Ports (the new interface, Resource interface, will be created with a Flow Property).

The validation rules are updated according to all changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The approach for working with ports changed after the migration from UPDM2, as outlined below:</p><ol><li>For all UPDM2 ports (Node Port and Resource Port), new Operational / Resource Interfaces are created and set as the port types. Separate interfaces are created for each of the ports. The name will be the same as the Type of the port with the keyword In, Out, or Inout added at the end according to the Flow Properties summarized in #2 below (for example, Data In). The location of the keywords will be next to the owner of the port. The new Operational / Resource Interface will be applied as a Type for port. No interface will be created if a port has no type.</li><li>Flow Properties are created for all the Interfaces (Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces). The Flow Property type is a Conveyed Item that is conveyed by the connector. The Flow Property direction is specified according to the information flow or relation direction from the current port perspective. If there is no Conveyed Item on the connector between ports, the Flow Property is created, the Port Type from UPDM2 is set as a type, and the direction is inout. If the Types of Ports are the same, the same interface can be reused. The new Flow Property will not be created if the type was Service Interface in UPDM2.</li><li>If Service / Request Ports interfaces are already created, they also have operations. Flow Properties will be created according to the parameters of these operations. The same number of Flow Properties and operations will have parameters with the same types. The Flow property direction will be the same as the parameter direction.</li><li>Services Access is migrated to Resource Service for Request/Service Ports (the new interface, Resource interface, will be created with a Flow Property).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68272549-bbab-4fbb-abfe-4c68ad685748"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The validation rules are updated according to all changes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00870: Pr Configuration Management

- page_id: `191927333`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927333/Pr+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.480+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['pr-configuration-management', 'pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

830957257

830957260

830957258

**Description**

The Pr Configuration Management view depicts the whole lifecycle view of a Resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The Pr view provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of different versions of Resources (usually Capability Configurations or Service Implementations) mapped against a timeline.

The Pr view may be used for:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Pr view can be represented using:

- Pr Configuration Management diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR2'] chapter.

- Pr MS Word document report.

830957255

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928325 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00871: Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `191928325`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928325/Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-22T12:22:32.827+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Coverage analysis
- labels: ['metric-suites', 'capabilities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-opactivities', 'nodes-covered-by-sysresources', 'opactivitie-covered-by-functions', 'opactivities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-sysinterfaces', 'capabilities-covered-by-capconfiguration', 'capabilities-covered-by-projects', 'orgresources-covered-by-projects', 'projects-covered-by-milestones', 'functions-covered-by-sysresources']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.

See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites hierarchy.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects

The Metric Suite "Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects" counts the percentage of the Actual Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Actual Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Functions Covered by Resource Performers

The Metric Suite "Functions Covered by Resource Performers" counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Functions, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Actual Projects Covered by Milestones

The Metric Suite "Projects Covered by Milestones" counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources

The Metric Suite "Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources" counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Organization Resources in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Organization Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Operational Activities Covered by Functions

The Metric Suite "Operational Activities Covered by Functions" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers

The Metric Suite "Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers

The Metric Suite "Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers" counts the percentage of the Operational Performers covered by Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Performers, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Actual Project

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.

##### Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Requirement)

Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations. 
Each requirement is treated as separate element:

- If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not treated as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.
- The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation grouping, the requirement is not treated as satisfied unless it also has an incoming satisfy relation.

##### Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Grouping Element)

Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations. 
Grouping requirement is not treated as independent requirement:

- If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are treated as covered independent of whether have they incoming satisfy relations or not.
- The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation, then the grouping requirement is treated as satisfied.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.</p><p>See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites hierarchy.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Metric Suite hierarchy" ac:alt="Metric Suite hierarchy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MetricSuite_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Predefined Metric Suites" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Actual Organizational Resources Covered by Actual Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Actual Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Functions Covered by Resource Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Functions Covered by Resource Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Functions, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.<br /></span></p><h3>Actual Projects Covered by Milestones</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Projects Covered by Milestones&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.<br /></span></p><h3>Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Organization Resources in the particular package. The number of  Actual Projects,  Actual Organization Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Operational Activities Covered by Functions</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Operational Activities Covered by Functions&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Performers covered by Resource Performers in the particular package. The number of Operational Performers, Resource Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Actual Project</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Operational Activities&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Capability Configuration&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Operational Performers&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Performers in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Performers, and the coverage average are also counted.</span></p><h3>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Requirement)</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations.<br />Each requirement is treated as separate element:</span></p><ul><li style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not treated as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation grouping, the requirement is not treated as satisfied unless it also has an incoming satisfy relation.<br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></li></ul><h3>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Grouping Element)</h3><p style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Metric suite tracks requirements coverage with design - checks incoming satisfy relations and requirements coverage with test cases - checks incoming verify relations.<br />Grouping requirement is not treated as independent requirement:</span></p><ul><li style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are treated as covered independent of whether have they incoming satisfy relations or not.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The same thing applies in the opposite direction. If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relation, then the grouping requirement is treated as satisfied.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929263 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00873: Preserving Sub Projects in PV-2

- page_id: `191929263`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929263/Preserving+Sub+Projects+in+PV-2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T11:08:59.334+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

If Actual Project type is read-only, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using To Do property' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in UAF.

**Solution**

To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to editablebefore the migration process.

If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy that type to current project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Issue</strong></p><p>If Actual Project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">type is read-only</span>, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using To Do property" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To DO property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12d22ee9-61cb-4c74-ab88-d3a68a71b98c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in <span>UAF</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Solution</strong></p><p>To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">editable </span>before the migration process.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eadc2f9b-a48c-45ad-9643-89d8737271f7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that type</span> to current project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929243 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00874: Preserving Sub Projects in PV-2 in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `191929243`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929243/Preserving+Sub+Projects+in+PV-2+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T11:09:25.401+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

If Actual Project type is read-only, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using To Do property' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in UAF.

**Solution**

To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to editablebefore the migration process.

If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy that type to current project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Issue</strong></p><p>If Actual Project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">type is read-only</span>, its Sub Projects do not migrate in PV-2 diagram. There is no possibility to create a Sub Projects for Actual Projects that type is read only element. Anyway, all non migrated Sub Projects of each Actual Project are listed in Specification window <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using To Do property" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To DO property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12d22ee9-61cb-4c74-ab88-d3a68a71b98c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is possible to create a Sub Projects for Actual projects which types are not read only in <span>UAF</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Solution</strong></p><p>To avoid this issue, it is recommended to change the Actual Project type from read only to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">editable </span>before the migration process.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eadc2f9b-a48c-45ad-9643-89d8737271f7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If the type is used from another project, you need to move/copy <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that type</span> to current project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928160 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00875: Preview Affected Diagrams dialog

- page_id: `191928160`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928160/Preview+Affected+Diagrams+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-15T10:55:40.954+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog is dedicated to review the diagrams which will be affected when the <viewpoint name>* Exchange will be realized. The dialog can be opened from the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Preview Affected Diagrams dialog contains all the affected diagrams. You can preview them one by one. The affected elements where the created Exchanges will be realized, are marked in the diagram preview pane.

While previewing the diagrams, you can select the **Update Diagram** check box for those diagrams you want to show the Conveyed Items of the selected exchange.

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The <strong>Preview Affected Diagrams</strong> dialog is dedicated to review the diagrams which will be affected when the &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange will be realized. The dialog can be opened from the following:</span></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="preview_affected_v2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Preview Affected Diagrams dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The Preview Affected Diagrams dialog contains all the affected diagrams. A property associated with a logical architecture, used to specify the scope of the problem.

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928163 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00878: Producing and Consuming Activities dialog

- page_id: `191928163`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928163/Producing+and+Consuming+Activities+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-07T14:10:58.642+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-activities-dialog', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog allows you to review and modify Producing and Consuming Activities for a selected Operational Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Operational Exchange, select Producing Activity / Consuming Activity property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the Operational Connectivity Table or Operational Role-based Connectivity Table, select Producing Operational Activity / Consuming Operational Activity cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog , select Producing Operational Activity / Consuming Operational Activity cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Activities pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Activity pairs

1. Select the needed Activity pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

1005096879

INLINE

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

## PAGE 00879: Producing and Consuming Functions dialog

- page_id: `191928167`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928167/Producing+and+Consuming+Functions+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-07T14:05:08.205+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-functions-dialog', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog allows you to review and modify the list of Producing and Consuming Functions for a selected Resource Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Resource Exchange, select Producing Function / Consuming Function property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the Resources Connectivity Table, Resource Role-based Connectivity Table, Personnel Connectivity Table, Personnel Role-based Connectivity Table, Security Connectivity Table, or Security Role-based Connectivity Table, select Producing Function / Consuming Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager dialog** , Select Producing Function / Consuming Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Functions pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Function pairs

1. Select the needed Functions pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928171 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00880: Producing and Consuming Service Functions dialog

- page_id: `191928171`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928171/Producing+and+Consuming+Service+Functions+dialog

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog allows you to review and modify the list of Producing and Consuming Service Functions for a selected Service Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Service Functions** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Resource Exchange, select Producing Service Function / Consuming Service Function property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the Services Connectivity Table or Service Role-based Connectivity Table, select Producing Service Function / Consuming Service Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Service Exchange Manager dialog** , Select Producing Service Function / Consuming Service Function cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Service Functions pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs

1. Select the needed Service Functions pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the Services Connectivity Table or Service Role-based Connectivity Table, select <strong>Producing Service Function</strong>/<strong> Consuming Service ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong> Service Exchange Manager dialog</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>, Select <strong>Producing Service Function</strong> / <strong>Consuming Service Function</strong> cell and click<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Service Functions dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span>.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td>Remove From Table</td><td>Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Service Functions pair.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Show Full Paths</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Show Full Paths button in Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" ac:alt="Show Full Paths button in Producing and Consuming Functions dialog"><ri:attachment Service Functions pair.</li><li>Click the <strong>Remove From Table</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929277 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00881: Products Mapping

- page_id: `191929277`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929277/Products+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-06-19T12:28:14.605+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x > DoDAF Projects Migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Products mapping converts DoDAF products into UPDM ones. It changes a product's name and representation form whenever required by the UPDM 1.1 plugin. DoDAF product migration is described in details in Table 1 on page 6 below.

| DoDAF 1.5 | UPDM 1.x |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Product | Representation Form | Product | Representation Form |
| OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic | UML Class Diagram | OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic | UML Composite Structure Diagram |
| OV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description | UML Class Diagram | OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description | UML Class Diagram |
| OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix | Report | OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix | Table |
| OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart | UML Class Diagram | OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart | UML Class Diagram |
| OV-5 Operational Activity Model | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram | OV-5 Operational Activity Model | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram |
| OV-6a Operational Rules Model | Report | OV-6a Operational Rules Model | Table |
| OV-6b Operational State Transition Description | UML State Machine Diagram | OV-6b Operational State Transition Description | UML State Machine Diagram |
| OV-6c Operational Event-TraceDescription | UML Sequence Diagram | OV-6c Operational Event-TraceDescription | UML Sequence Diagram |
| OV-7 Logical Data Model | UML Class Diagram | OV-7 Information Model | UML Class Diagram |
| SV-1 Systems Interface Description | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram | SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram |
| SV-2 Systems Communications Description | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram | SV-2 Resource Communicationsdescription | UML Class DiagramUML Composite Structure Diagram |
| SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix | Matrix | SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix | Matrix |
| SV-4 Systems Functionality Description | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram | SV-4 Functionality Description | UML Class DiagramUML Activity Diagram |
| SV-5 Operational Activity to SystemsFunction Traceability Matrix | Matrix | SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix | Editable Matrix |
| SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix | Report | SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix | Table |
| SV-7 Systems Performance Parameters Matrix | Table | SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix | Actual and Typical Tables |
| SV-8 Systems Evolution Description | UML State Machine Diagram | SV-8 Capability Configuration Management | UML Class Diagram |
| SV-9 Systems Technology Forecast | Table | SV-9 Technology and Skills Forecast | Table |
| SV-10a Systems Rules Model | Report | SV-10a Resource ConstraintsSpecification | Table |
| SV-10b Systems State Transition Description | UML State Machine Diagram | SV-10b Resource State TransitionDescription | UML State Machine Diagram |
| SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description | UML Sequence Diagram | SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description | UML Sequence Diagram |
| SV-11 Physical Schema | UML Class Diagram | SV-11 Physical Schema | UML Class Diagram |
| TV-1 Technical Standards Profile | Table | Does not map | - |
| TV-2 Technical Standards Forecast | Table | TV-2 Standards Forecast | Table |

Do not be surprised, if you will not find any OV-3, OV-6a, SV-6 and SV-10a reports after migrating to UPDM 1.x. These reports do not exist in UPDM 1.x. There are tables instead. They can be easily filled with data, conveniently updated, and printed as any other MagicDraw diagrams.

These tables, however, will not be created automatically after migrating to UPDM 1.x. We suggest you to create them manually by selecting the migrated data you would like to see in each one.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It changes a product's name and representation form whenever required by the UPDM 1.1 plugin. DoDAF product migration is described in details in Table 1 on page 6 below.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 81.2401%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 27.62%;" /><col style="width: - labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An element that describes types of time-limited endeavors that are required to meet one or more Capability needs.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Class

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929062 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00885: Project Kind

- page_id: `191929062`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929062/Project+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:55:17.801+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of project applicable to an ActualProject. Its enumeration literals are:

- Programme - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an undertaking that is a temporary, flexible organization created to co-ordinate, direct and oversee the implementation of a set of related Projects and Tasks in order to deliver outcomes and benefits related to the organization’s strategic objectives. A programme is likely to have a lifespan of several years. During a programme lifecycle, projects are initiated, executed, and closed. Programmes provide an umbrella under which these projects can be co-ordinated. The programme integrates the projects so that it can deliver an outcome greater than the sum of its parts.
- Portfolio - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an undertaking comprised of the Projects and Programmes that are the totality of an organization's investment (or segment thereof) in the changes required to achieve its strategic objectives.
- Project - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an undertaking that is a time-limited endeavor to create a specific set of products or services.
- PersonnelDevelopment - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an undertaking that relates to the training and enablement of personnel to enable them help achieve the organizations objectives.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of project applicable to an ActualProject. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>Programme - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an undertaking that is a temporary, flexible organization created to co-ordinate, direct and oversee the implementation of a set of related Projects and Tasks in order to deliver outcomes and benefits related to the organization’s strategic objectives. A programme is likely to have a lifespan of several years. During a programme lifecycle, projects are initiated, executed, and closed. Programmes provide an umbrella under which these projects can be co-ordinated. The programme integrates the projects so that it can deliver an outcome greater than the sum of its parts.</li><li>Portfolio - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an  undertaking comprised of the Projects and Programmes that are the totality of an organization's investment (or segment thereof) in the changes required to achieve its strategic objectives.</li><li>Project - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an undertaking that is a time-limited endeavor to create a specific set of products or services.</li><li>PersonnelDevelopment - Indicates that the ActualProject associated with the ProjectKind is an undertaking that relates to the training and enablement of personnel to enable them help achieve the organizations objectives.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929183 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00886: Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `191929183`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929183/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:05.863+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1159356519

1159356540

1159356518

**On this page**

1159356530

There are some issues with the Teamwork Cloud projects migration to UAF 1.2 version when those projects contain used projects:

- Used projects are not migrated.
- Used projects are migrated, but not updated to the latest version on Teamwork Cloud.

##### Issues of manual projects migration

Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. [Learn more about roles and permissions >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions)

###### Used projects are not migrated

If you are trying to migrate a project containing not migrated used projects, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not migrated. To proceed with the migration of the main project, you need to migrate the used projects mentioned in the notification message first.

###### Used projects are migrated, but not updated

If you are trying to migrate a project containing used projects not updated to the latest version, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not updated. In this case, you can choose from the following options:

- Update and Migrate. Used projects will be updated to the latest version, and the project will be migrated. This option is strongly recommended.
- Migrate. The main project will be migrated without updating the used projects to the latest version. In this case, the used projects will remain outdated, and you will not be able to work with them.
- Open as Read-Only. The migration is canceled, and the project opens as read-only. In this case, the project will not be migrated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more information about manual project migration, see [Migrating server projects manually](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsmanually).

##### Issues of automatic projects migration

Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. [Learn more about roles and permissions >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions)

If the main project contains used projects, you need to migrate the used projects first. Then you can perform the automatic migration of the main project. After the migration is complete, you need to switch your used project versions to the latest (if the latest versions were used before migration).

When you migrate projects automatically and receive a message that some projects were not migrated, it might be that those projects are using other projects. In this case, do one of the following:

- Migrate the listed projects manually.
- Repeat the automatic migration.

For more information about automatic project migration, see [Migrating server projects automatically](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically).

##### Roles and permissions

For the migration of server projects, certain roles and permissions are needed. Before migrating, make sure you have **Resource Locks Administrator** and **Resource Manager roles.**Also, you need to have **Administer Resources** and **Release Resource Locks** permissions.

To learn more, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2022x'].

For more information about migrating server projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migrating Server Projects' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

##### Special migration options

There are special migration options for the server projects:

- The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the magicdraw.properties (or cea.properties ) file and change the value of the system property -Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to.uaf\=false to True . By default, the value of this property is False , which means, that your UPDM projects will not be migrated.

[Learn more>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically)

- You can suspend the used projects updating by adding **-Dsuspend.modules.update.during.migration\=false** in the properties file and set it to *true*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ee19ab4-905d-4267-963f-c07be63b2fdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1159356519</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58101d6e-fa36-403b-906f-a189d95a302f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1159356540</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c349dbb3-8c3b-41f9-a425-75f3820ef987"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1159356518</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4490863-ff0b-4380-b0fb-c26b8652fb93" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4314ff6-e4e7-4ffe-831f-a8cda4f532ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1159356530</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are some issues with the Teamwork Cloud projects migration to UAF 1.2 version when those projects contain used projects:</p><ul><li>Used projects are not migrated.</li><li>Used projects are migrated, but not updated to the latest version on Teamwork Cloud.</li></ul><h3>Issues of manual projects migration</h3><p>Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions">Learn more about roles and permissions &gt;&gt;</a></p><h4>Used projects are not migrated</h4><p>If you are trying to migrate a project containing not migrated used projects, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not migrated. To proceed with the migration of the main project, you need to migrate the used projects mentioned in the notification message first.</p><h4>Used projects are migrated, but not updated</h4><p>If you are trying to migrate a project containing used projects not updated to the latest version, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not updated. In this case, you can choose from the following options:</p><ul><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Update and Migrate.</strong> Used projects will be updated to the latest version, and the project will be migrated. This option is <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">strongly recommended.</strong></li><li><strong>Migrate.</strong> The main project will be migrated without updating the used projects to the latest version. In this case, the used projects will remain outdated, and you will not be able to work with them.</li><li><strong>Open as Read-Only.</strong> The migration is canceled, and the project opens as read-only. In this case, the project will not be migrated.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pic2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c23af08a-a384-4796-96e7-915a630379f1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about manual project migration, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsmanually">Migrating server projects manually</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Issues of automatic projects migration</h3><p>Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions">Learn more about roles and permissions &gt;&gt;</a></p><p>If the main project contains used projects, you need to migrate the used projects first. Then you can perform the automatic migration of the main project. After the migration is complete, you need to switch your used project versions to the latest (if the latest versions were used before migration).</p><p>When you migrate projects automatically and receive a message that some projects were not migrated, it might be that those projects are using other projects. In this case, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Migrate the listed projects manually.</li><li>Repeat the automatic migration. </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0999d47e-f1b2-4015-a180-a5101015706a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about automatic project migration, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically">Migrating server projects automatically</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Roles and permissions </h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">For the migration of server projects, certain roles and permissions are needed. Before migrating, make sure you have <strong>Resource Locks Administrator</strong> and <strong>Resource Manager roles. </strong>Also, you need to have <strong>Administer Resources</strong> and <strong>Release Resource Locks</strong> permissions.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To learn more, see<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2022x" ri:content-title="Permissions" /></ac:link>.</p><p>For more information about migrating server projects, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Migrating Server Projects" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</p><h3>Special migration options</h3><p>There are special migration options for the server projects:</p><ul><li>The UPDM project migration from the modeling tool with the UAF environment will not be performed. However, if you want to migrate the UPDM project to UAF, you need to open the<span> </span><strong>magicdraw.properties</strong><span> </span>(or<span> </span><strong>cea.properties</strong>) file and change the value of the system property<span> </span><strong>-Dmigrate.project.from.updm2.to.uaf\=false</strong><span> </span>to<span> </span><em>True</em>. By default, the value of this property is<span> </span><em>False</em>, which means, that your UPDM projects will not be migrated.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically">Learn more&gt;&gt;</a></p><ul><li>You can suspend the used projects updating by adding <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>-Dsuspend.modules.update.during.migration\=false</strong> in the properties file and set it to <em>true</em>.</span> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928176 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00892: Project Status Application Wizard

- page_id: `191928176`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928176/Project+Status+Application+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-26T12:32:50.727+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['project-status-application-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1011370957

1011370959

1011370958

The **Project Status Application Wizard** is used to apply the DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or custom Milestone type to the selected element, which can be one of the following milestone kinds:

- In Service
- Deployed
- No Longer Used
- Out Of Service
- Other

To open the **Project Status Application Wizard**

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The Project Status Application Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of two steps:

truenone

##### Actual Project Milestone Type selection

In this step, select DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or create your own Milestone type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Project Status at a particular Milestone specification

In this step, specify project threads and status indicators of the selected Actual Project Milestone Type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

|  | Project threads | Status indicators |
| --- | --- | --- |
| DLOD Milestone type | EquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTraining | DLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical Issues |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

|  | Project threads | Status indicators |
| --- | --- | --- |
| DOTMLPF Milestone type | DoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilities | Materiel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & Support |

If you selected your own milestone type, you can create your own projects threads and status indicators.

To create project threads and status indicators

1. In the Project Thread area, click the Add button.
2. Specify the thread name.
3. In the Status Indicator area, click the Add button.
4. Specify the indicator name.
5. Click the Fill Color button.
6. In the Color dialog choose the color and click OK .

To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the **Clone**button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.

To remove the applied Project Status

- In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .

1011370955

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Project Status in Lr diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing PV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Project Status from Lr diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a77026d9-d546-41f6-bd65-d207afcfd86a"><ac:parameter ri:filename="project_status_application_wizard_step2_DOTMLPF.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table type</strong></td><td><ul><li>Doctrine</li><li>Organization</li><li>Training</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Leadership</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Facilities</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Materiel Solution Thread area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Specify the thread name.</li><li>In the Status Indicator area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Specify the indicator ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fff6feea-1be7-46d7-9c13-982f09272d1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the <strong>Clone </strong>button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove the applied Project Status</p><hr /><ul><li>In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a272a350-6321-4504-b74d-c72c7e368ad6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1011370955</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Edit Project Status in Lr diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing PV-2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Removing Project Status from Lr diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926531 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00894: Project viewpoint

- page_id: `191926531`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926531/Project+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:12:53.878+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['project-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305730323

305730325

305730324

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Project Viewpoint describe how programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to them, and dependencies between them. Previous versions of DoDAF took a traditional model of architecture in which descriptions of programs and projects were considered outside scope. To compensate for this, various DoDAF models represented the evolution of systems, technologies and standards (e.g., Systems and Services Evolution Description, Systems Technology Forecast, and Technical Standards Forecast).” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

305730322

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f669f29-399a-4e97-8401-4f35e0431b22"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305730323</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f42f171b-34ad-4b75-80bf-3cf7d8046667"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305730325</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4eb0c97-4646-4be6-b213-337002aee1af"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305730324</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>“The DoDAF-described Models within the Project Viewpoint describe how programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to them, and dependencies between them. Previous versions of DoDAF took a traditional model of architecture in which descriptions of programs and projects were considered outside scope. To compensate for this, various DoDAF models represented the evolution of systems, technologies and standards (e.g., Systems and Services Evolution Description, Systems Technology Forecast, and Technical Standards Forecast).” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df353d91-8ee4-4977-b371-d1abf38a2edd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305730322</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="42db5711-c6c6-4926-b4c7-727698cdb705" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00896: Projects Connectivity

- page_id: `191927983`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927983/Projects+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:02:05.329+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

912913740

912913742

912913741

**Description**

The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain shows the relationships between projects and project milestones.

The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

The Projects Connectivity (Pj-Cn) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Connectivity diagram

**Description**

The Projects Parameters (Pr-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Projects Parameters (Pr-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

- Actual Measurements (.xlsx)

## PAGE 00899: Projects Roadmap

- page_id: `191927995`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927995/Projects+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:20:58.428+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913359236

913359239

913359238

**Description**

The Projects Roadmap (Pj-Rm) domain shows the product portfolio management; planning of capability delivery. It provides a timeline perspective on programs or projects. This domain is not limited to the acquisition and fielding processes.

The intended usage of the Projects Roadmap (Pj-Rm) domain includes:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependency risk identification.
- Management of dependencies.
- Portfolio management.

**Implementation**

The Projects Roadmap (Pj-Rm) domain is represented by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Roadmap diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928001 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00900: Projects Structure

- page_id: `191928001`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928001/Projects+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:57:46.016+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913540941

913540943

913540942

**Description**

The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain shows relationships between types of projects and project milestones. It provides a template for an actual project(s) road map(s) to be implemented. The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

The Projects Structure (Pj-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Structure diagram

## PAGE 00901: Projects Taxonomy

- page_id: `191928017`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:51:53.211+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913814663

913814665

913814664

**Description**

The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain shows the taxonomy of types of projects and project milestones. It represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Projects Taxonomy Table

This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.</p><p>The intended usage of the The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes, but is not limited to:</p><ul><li>Program management (specified acquisition program structure).</li><li>Project organization.</li><li>Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Projects Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Projects Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Projects Taxonomy table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.
