<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928030 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00902: Projects Traceability

- page_id: `191928030`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928030/Projects+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:25:39.786+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913969804

913969806

913969805

**Description**

The Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain shows the traceability between capabilities and projects that deliver them.The Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.

Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.

The intended usage of the Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to projects.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

The Projects Traceability (Pj-Tr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Project Activities to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .

**Samples**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of theProject Activities to Capabilities Mapping matrix

913969803

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928478 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00905: Property Set elements for measurement tables

- page_id: `191928478`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928478/Property+Set+elements+for+measurement+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-08-29T11:18:52.111+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Working with measurement tables, you can create or add Property Sets. The list of available Property Sets according to the domain is provided in the following table:

| Measurement Tables | Property Sets and their specializations |
| --- | --- |
| Strategic Measurement Tables | CapabilityChallengeDriverEffectEnterprise GoalEnterprise ObjectiveEnterprise VisionMeasurement Set or their specializationsOpportunityStrategic InformationStrategic PhaseValue Item |
| Operational Measurement Tables | High Level Operational ConceptKnown ResourceMeasurement Set (except Value Item)Operational ArchitectureOperational InformationOperational InterfaceOperational MitigationOperational PerformerOperational Signal |
| Services Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)ServiceService Architecture Service InterfaceService Signal |
| Personnel Measurement Tables | CompetenceMeasurement Set (except Value Item)OrganizationPersonPostProjectResponsibility |
| Resources Measurement Tables | Capability ConfigurationKnown ResourceMeasurement Set (except Value Item)Natural ResourceOrganizationPersonPostProjectResource ArchitectureResource ArtifactResource InformationResource InterfaceResource MitigationResource ServiceResource Service InterfaceResource SignalResponsibilitySecurity EnclaveSoftwareSystemTechnologyWhole Life Configuration |
| Security Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)Operational MitigationResource MitigationRiskSecurity EnclaveSecurity Risk |
| Project Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)ProjectProject Milestone |
| Standards Measurement Tables | Measurement Set (except Value Item)ProtocolProtocol StackStandardTechnology |
| Actual Resources Typical Measurement Tables | CompetenceMeasurement Set (except Value Item)OrganizationPersonPostProject Responsibility |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929071 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00907: Protects In Context

- page_id: `191929071`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929071/Protects+In+Context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:53:12.825+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A dependency relationship that relates a Security Control Action to a Operational Role, or a Resource Role. It indicates that Security Control is required to protect an Asset in a specific context or configuration.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Dependency

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926532 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00915: PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships

- page_id: `191926532`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926532/PV-1+Project+Portfolio+Relationships
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:16:09.566+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-1', 'pv-1-project-portfolio-relationships', 'pv-1-responsibility-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305775065

305775067

305775066

**Description**

The PV-1 represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives.

The PV-1 enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The PV-1 provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the PV-1 includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

PV-1 can be represented using:

- PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='pv_1.png']
- PV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='pv_1_matrix.png']

The model is strongly linked with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-4 Capability Dependencies' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model which shows capability groupings and dependencies.

305775064

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Responsible For' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Status Indicators' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Post' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Person' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="866849cf-4c95-40ca-ab07-4d92562930a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305775065</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0266a641-8f8c-4a18-8cc3-316e2a42f3db"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305775067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e24860fc-4793-4ba7-8656-475b04f2797a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305775066</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The PV-1 represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives.</p><p>The PV-1 enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The PV-1 provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.</p><p>The intended usage of the PV-1 includes, but is not limited to:</p><ul><li>Program management (specified acquisition program structure).</li><li>Project organization.</li><li>Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>PV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" ac:alt="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pv_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>PV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix.<br /><ac:image ac:title="PV-1 Responsibility Matrix" ac:alt="PV-1 Responsibility Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pv_1_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul><p>The model is strongly linked with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-4 Capability Dependencies" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> model which shows capability groupings and dependencies.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d525703-99aa-40b4-9374-c3c70d4998bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305775064</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Milestone" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Sequence" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Milestone Dependency" Indicators" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Organization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating PV-1 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926537 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00916: PV-2 Project Timelines

- page_id: `191926537`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926537/PV-2+Project+Timelines
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T07:14:49.212+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-2', 'pv-2-project-timelines', 'pv-2-gantt-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305999026

305999028

305999027

**Description**

The PV-2 provides a timeline perspective on programs. The PV-2 is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of DoDD 5000.1 Defense Acquisition System policies to achieve a successfully integrated capability. The PV-2 is not limited to the acquisition and fielding processes.

The intended usage of the PV-2 includes:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependency risk identification.
- Management of dependencies.
- Portfolio management.

**Implementation**

PV-2 can be represented using a PV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use of PV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] model, if one exists.

305999025

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Milestone' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926542 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00917: PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `191926542`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926542/PV-3+Project+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:52.435+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-3', 'pv-3-project-to-capability-mapping', 'pv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306143199

306143202

306143200

**Description**

The PV-3 supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.

The PV-3 maps programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives to capabilities to show how the specific elements help to achieve a capability. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.

The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to projects.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], but provides the interface between Capability and Actual Project models rather than Operational to System Models.

306143198

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maps To Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating PV-3 matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.</p><p>The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to projects.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" ac:alt="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pv_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but provides the interface between Capability and Actual Project models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59709386-c38c-4256-bdd0-ea87e9076c2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306143198</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maps To Capability" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating PV-3 matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928181 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00918: Realized Operational Exchange wizard

- page_id: `191928181`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928181/Realized+Operational+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-08T14:16:52.712+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-operational-exchange-wizard', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard gives the instructions to follow when adding a new Operational Exchange or selecting one of the existing Operational Exchanges.

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Operational Exchange creation (mandatory)

This step is designed to create a new Operational Exchange by specifying a Conveyed Item, both sending and receiving Operational Agents,and a direction of an Operational Exchange. Depending on a fact, if the selected Operational Agentis known or not, requested information differs:

- If sending and receiving Operational Agents are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_operational_exchange_wizard_I_unknown_performers.png']
- If sending and receiving Operational Agent are known, they are added to a Realized Operational Exchange wizard automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_operational_activity_I.png']

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Conveyed Item | One or more Operational Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Operational Agents to receiving Operational Agents.Exchange KindConveyed ItemConfiguration ExchangeCapability ConfigurationEnergy ExchangeNatural ResourceGeo Political Extent ExchangeGeo Political Extent TypeInformation ExchangeInformation ElementOperational SignalMateriel ExchangeTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactOrganizational ExchangeProjectOrganizationPostResponsibilityPerson |
| Exchange Kind | Conveyed Item |
| Configuration Exchange | Capability Configuration |
| Energy Exchange | Natural Resource |
| Geo Political Extent Exchange | Geo Political Extent Type |
| Information Exchange | Information ElementOperational Signal |
| Materiel Exchange | TechnologySoftwareResource Artifact |
| Organizational Exchange | ProjectOrganizationPostResponsibilityPerson |
| Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified Conveyed Items.If you select an existing Operational Exchange from the drop-down list, the Conveyed Item box and Direction drop-down list will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit the values of Conveyed Items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Operational Agents are known from the wizard invocation context. |
| Sending Operational Agent | Operational Agents that send the specified conveyed information.Available if an Operational Agent is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Receiving Operational Agent | Operational Agents that receive the specified conveyed information.Available if an Operational Agent is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Direction | The direction of the information flow.After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Operational Agent is switched with the receiving Operational Agent. The direction of the Operational Exchange is changed accordingly. |
| Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships | Select the check box if you want to add the Operational Exchange to the corresponding relationships with the same sending and receiving Operational Agents at their ends as defined in the first step of the wizard. |

##### Producing and Consuming Activities editing

This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities performed by the Operational Agents. You can remove the realization between listed Operational Activity pairs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected diagram reviewing and updating

In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Operational Exchange can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified exchange to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Operational Exchange to be displayed.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams** controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified exchanges. Set the option to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Operational Exchanges whenever a new exchange is created or an existing one is modified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Internal Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

You will be able to edit the values of Conveyed Items.</p><p>The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c19b27c-5c5c-436f-bfaf-16508fe5dc2a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if sending and receiving Operational Agents<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>are known from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><th>Sending Operational Agent</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Agents </span>that send the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e410823-879a-4707-91fa-d97de1df96ae"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if an Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span>is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><th>Receiving Operational Agent</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Agents </span>that receive the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5376c484-70f6-4b6c-a220-8cff9fcdb490"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if an Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span>is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><th> Direction</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p> The direction of the information flow.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Specifying the direction" ac:alt="Specifying the direction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_direction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent </span>is switched with the receiving Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. The direction of the Operational Exchange is changed accordingly.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><th><p>Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships</p></th><td><p>Select the check box if you want to add the Operational Exchange to the corresponding relationships with the same sending and receiving Operational Agents at their ends as defined in the first step of the wizard.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Producing and Consuming Activities editing</h3><p>This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities performed by the Operational Agents. You can remove the realization between listed Operational Activity pairs.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_II.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Affected diagram reviewing and updating</h3><p>In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Operational Exchange can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified exchange to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Operational Exchange to be displayed. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24f81a89-6afa-40e8-9b18-a517ab8132b3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><strong> Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams</strong> controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified exchanges. Set the option to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Operational Exchanges whenever a new exchange is created or an existing one is modified.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_III.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8618a39-c3c2-4266-a035-571ad94ca213"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Preview Affected Diagrams dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928189 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00919: Realized Resource Exchange wizard

- page_id: `191928189`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928189/Realized+Resource+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:24:59.154+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-resource-interaction-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the **Realized****Resource Exchange** wizard

- On the diagram pane, select the relationship (e.g. Association) and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
- On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect the needed Resources.
- In the table toolbar, click the Add New button.
- In the matrix, right-click the intersection in the matrix and select Add New Resource Exchange .

The **Realized Resource****Exchange**wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Resource Exchange creation (mandatory)

This step is designed to create a new Resource Exchangeby specifying a conveyed Resource ExchangeItem, both sending and receiving Resources, and a direction of a Resource Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Resources are known or not, requested information differs:

- If sending and receiving Resources are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If sending and receiving Resources are known, they are added to a Realized Resource **Exchange** wizard automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Exchange Kind | Shows the type of exchanged Resources:Resource Communication.Resource Movement.Resource Energy Flow.Geo Political Extent Exchange. |
| Conveyed Item | One or more Resource Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Resource to receiving Resource.Exchange KindConveyed ItemGeo Political Extent ExchangeGeo Political Extent TypeResource CommunicationData ElementResource SignalResource Energy FlowNatural ResourceResource MovementSecurity EnclaveResource MitigationCapability ConfigurationSystemResource ArchitectureNatural ResourceTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactProjectResponsibilityPersonPostOrganizationKnown Resource |
| Exchange Kind | Conveyed Item |
| Geo Political Extent Exchange | Geo Political Extent Type |
| Resource Communication | Data ElementResource Signal |
| Resource Energy Flow | Natural Resource |
| Resource Movement | Security EnclaveResource MitigationCapability ConfigurationSystemResource ArchitectureNatural ResourceTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactProjectResponsibilityPersonPostOrganizationKnown Resource |
| Resource Exchange | Resource Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified Conveyed Items.If you select an existing Resource Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item box and Direction drop-down list will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of Conveyed Items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Resources are known from the wizard invocation context.Resource Exchange TypeSending and Receiving ResourcesCommandOrganizationPersonPostResponsibilityControlNatural ResourceOrganizational ResourceResource Artifact:Capability ConfigurationResource MitigationSecurity EnclaveSystemResource ExchangePhysical ResourceResource ArchitectureKnown Resource |
| Resource Exchange Type | Sending and Receiving Resources |
| Command | OrganizationPersonPostResponsibility |
| Control | Natural ResourceOrganizational ResourceResource Artifact:Capability ConfigurationResource MitigationSecurity EnclaveSystem |
| Resource Exchange | Physical ResourceResource ArchitectureKnown Resource |
| Sending Resource | Resource that sends the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Receiving Resource | Resource that receives the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Direction | Direction of the information flow.After changing the direction from the default one, the Sending Resource will be switched with the Receiving Resource. The direction of the Resource Exchange is changed accordingly.In the Direction drop-down list, you can choose the direction of flow, i.e. what type of element is the sender and what type of element is the receiver. The drop-down list is not applicable, if the source and target is the same element and direction could not be specified. |
| Add Resource Exchange to corresponding relationships | Select the check box if you want to add the Resource Exchange to the corresponding relationships with the same sending and receiving Resources at their ends as defined in the first step of the wizard. |

##### Producing and Consuming Functions editing

This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Functions for the created or selected Resource Exchange. ResourceExchanges are produced and consumed by Activities performed by the Resource. You can remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected diagram reviewing and updating

In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Resource Exchange can be realized and displayed.You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified exchange to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Resource Exchange to be displayed.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams** controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified exchanges. Set the option to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Resource Exchanges whenever a new exchange is created or an existing one is modified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928202 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00920: Realized Service Exchange wizard

- page_id: `191928202`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928202/Realized+Service+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:26:42.136+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-service-interaction-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the **Realized** **Service Exchange** wizard

- Do one of the following: - On the diagram pane, select the Service Association and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] . - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange_button.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect Services.

The **Realized Service** **Exchange**wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Service Exchange creation (mandatory)

This step is designed to create a new Service Exchangeby specifying a conveyed Service ExchangeItem, both sending and receiving Services, and a direction of a Service Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Services are known or not, requested information differs:

- If sending and receiving Services are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_service_exchange_unknown_services_step_I.png']
- If sending and receiving Services are known , they are added to a Realized Service **Exchange** wizard automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_service_exchange_known_services_step_I.png']

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Exchange Kind | Shows the type of exchanged items:Information ExchangeConfiguration ExchangeEnergy ExchangeMateriel ExchangeOrganizational Exchange |
| Conveyed Item | One or more Service Exchange Items to be conveyed by the information flow from the sending Service to receiving Service.Exchange KindConveyed ItemInformation ExchangeService SignalOperational InformationConfiguration ExchangeResource ArchitectureEnergy ExchangeNatural ResourceMateriel ExchangeResource ArtifactOrganizational ExchangeOrganizational Resources |
| Exchange Kind | Conveyed Item |
| Information Exchange | Service SignalOperational Information |
| Configuration Exchange | Resource Architecture |
| Energy Exchange | Natural Resource |
| Materiel Exchange | Resource Artifact |
| Organizational Exchange | Organizational Resources |
| Service Exchange | Service Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified Conveyed Items.If you select an existing Service Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item box and Direction drop-down list will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit the values of Conveyed Items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available, if sending and receiving Services are known from the wizard invocation context. |
| Sending Service | Service that sends the specified conveyed information.Available, if a Service is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Receiving Service | Service that receives the specified conveyed information.Available, if a Service is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Direction | The direction of the information flow.After changing the direction from the default one, the Sending Service will be switched with the Receiving Service. The direction of the Service Exchange is changed accordingly.In the Direction drop-down list, you can choose the direction of flow, i.e. what type of element is the sender and what type of element is the receiver. The drop-down list is not applicable if the source and target are the same element and the direction cannot be specified. |
| Add Service Exchange to corresponding relationships | Select the check box if you want to add the Service Exchange to the corresponding relationships with the same sending and receiving Services at their ends as defined in the first step of the wizard. |

##### Producing and Consuming Service Functions editing

This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Service Functions for the created or selected Service Exchange. Service Exchanges are produced and consumed by Service Functions performed by the Services. You can remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected diagram reviewing and updating

In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Service Exchange can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified exchange to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Service Exchange to be displayed.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] **Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams** controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified exchanges. Set the option to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Service Exchanges whenever a new exchange is created or an existing one is modified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Service Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S2 Service Internal Structure' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928210 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00921: Realized Strategic Exchange wizard

- page_id: `191928210`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928210/Realized+Strategic+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:28:01.422+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-service-interaction-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the **Realized** **Strategic Exchange** wizard

- Do one of the following: - On the diagram pane, select the Actual Enterprise Phase and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_exchange_button.png'] . - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_exchange_button_pallete.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect Actual Enterprise Phases.

The **Realized Strategic** **Exchange**wizard consists of only one step:

truenone

##### Strategic Exchange creation

In this step, you create a new Strategic Exchangeby specifying its name, conveyed Item, and direction of a Strategic Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can choose one or more Conveyed Items from the list or create new ones. When creating a new Conveyed Item, choose the type depending on the viewpoint:

| Viewpoint | Conveyed Item Type |
| --- | --- |
| Strategic | Strategic Information |
| Security | Security Enclave |
| Resource Mitigation |  |
| Resources | System |
| Software |  |
| Resource Artifact |  |
| Technology |  |
| Natural Resource |  |
| Resource Service |  |
| Resource Architecture |  |
| Capability Configuration |  |
| Projects | Project |
| Personnel | Post |
|  | Responsibility |
|  | Person |
|  | Organization |
| Parameters | Geo Political Extent Type |
| Operational | Know Resource |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>To open the <span class="confluence-link"> <strong>Realized </strong> <strong>Strategic Exchange</strong> wizard</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<br />- On the diagram pane, select the Actual Enterprise Phase and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_exchange_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />- Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_exchange_button_pallete.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Enterprise Phases.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Realized Strategic </strong> <strong>Exchange </strong>wizard consists of only one step:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f88aef8d-1a7b-4fda-b7a2-c72f73194f16"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Strategic Exchange creation</h3><p>In this step, you create a new Strategic <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>by specifying its name, conveyed Item, and direction of a Strategic <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_strategic_exchange_wizard.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You can choose one or more Conveyed Items from the list or create new ones. When creating a new Conveyed Item, choose the type depending on the viewpoint:</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 21.6127%;"><colgroup class=""> <col class="" style="width: 35.369%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 64.631%;" /> </colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Viewpoint</th><th>Conveyed Item Type</th></tr><tr class=""><th>Strategic</th><td>Strategic Information</td></tr><tr class=""><th rowspan="2">Security</th><td>Security Enclave</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Resource Mitigation</td></tr><tr class=""><th rowspan="8">Resources</th><td colspan="1">System</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Software</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Resource Artifact</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Technology</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Natural Resource</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><p>Resource Service</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Resource Architecture</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Capability Configuration</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Projects</th><td colspan="1">Project</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Personnel</th><td colspan="1">Post</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1"><br /></th><td colspan="1">Responsibility</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1"><br /></th><td colspan="1">Person</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1"><br /></th><td colspan="1">Organization</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Parameters</th><td colspan="1">Geo Political Extent Type</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Operational</th><td colspan="1">Know Resource</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926950 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00922: Relating actual projects in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926950`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926950/Relating+actual+projects+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-19T09:55:05.691+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.

You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate actual projects

1. Select two actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between actual projects

1. Select two related actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p><br /></p><p>To relate actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation between actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the suggested solutions.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="NPV-1_relate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Relating actual projects in NPV-1 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="891e99ec-42d8-498a-9fe8-50471d67eb75"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927181 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00923: Relating Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `191927181`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927181/Relating+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:08:16.004+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['relate-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate projects

1. Select two Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between projects

1. Select two related Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li><p>Select one of the suggested solutions.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-2_Validation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Relating Projects in Lr diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928373 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00924: Relationship mapping

- page_id: `191928373`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928373/Relationship+mapping
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.018+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Importing from ArchiMate
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

195753003

195753014

195753004

How ArchiMate relationships are mapped to the UAF 1.2 relationships or added to UAF element properties after the import and the mapping rules are described in the following table:

| # | ArchiMate 3.1 relation | UAF 1.2 | Mapping Rule |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | Type |  |  |  |
| 1 | Access | ReadWriteAccessRead/Write | Flow Property | The ArchiMate Access is mapped to UAF Flow Property which is applied to Interface when the ArchiMate Access relation is used to relate the ArchiMate element to Business Interface/Technology Interface/Application Interface.Access Type is set as the direction for Flow Property: Read - in,Write - out,Access - inout,ReadWrite - inout.Also, see Mapping issues. |
|  | Dependency | The ArchiMate Access is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Access relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |
| 2 | Aggregation | Association | Common RuleCommon Rule - after the import to UAF maps to Aggregation (Association with Aggregation = Shared) |  |
| Physical Location property | The Location information is added to the UAF Physical Location element property if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates the Actual Location to the Location Holder (i.e. Operational Performer, Resource Performer).No relation is created in UAF. |  |  |  |
| Link | The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Link [InstanceSpecification] if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates elements that map to the Instance Specifications with the same type. |  |  |  |
| Trace | The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Trace if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Aggregation relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 3 | Assignment | Is Capable To Perform | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Is Capable To Perform if the ArchiMate Assignment relates the ArchiMate elements that map to UAF Operational Performer and Operational Activity or Resource Performer and Function. |  |
| Resource Port | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Resource Port when Application Interface/Technology Interface is assigned to Application Service/Technology Interface.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Service Port | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Service Port if Business Interface is assigned to Business Service.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Assignment relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 4 | Association | isDirected=true | Association | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Association with the navigable end (the source and target are the same as in ArchiMate) |
| isDirected=False | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Association without the navigable end (the source and target are the same as in ArchiMate) |  |  |  |
|  | Is Capable To Perform | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Is Capable To Perform relation if ArchiMate Resource is related to Business Process. |  |  |
| Link | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to UAF Link [InstanceSpecification] if the ArchiMate Association relates elements that map to the Instance Specifications with the same type. |  |  |  |
| Actual Project Milestone Role | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Actual Project Milestone Role [Slot] when the ArchiMAte Association relates Workpackage to Deliverable/Implementation Event. |  |  |  |
| Trace | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Trace if the ArchiMate Association relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Association relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 5 | Composition | Association | Common Rule - after import to UAF maps to Composition (Association with Aggregation = Composite) |  |
| Containment | The ArchiMate Composition is mapped to UAF Containment if the ArchiMate Composition relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Slot | If ArchiMate Composition relate elements that in UAF become Actual Measurement Sets, then in UAF Composition relation is not created, but "main" Actual Measurement Set is created as owner which owns "inner" Actual Measurement Sets (owner with slot subMeasurementType and values for this slot are created) |  |  |  |
| 6 | Connection (Line) | UAF Element onto Information → Annotated Element | The ArchiMate Connection/Line/Anchor information is added to the Annotated Element property of the UAF Information element. |  |
| 7 | Flow | Operational Exchange | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Operational Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF elements from the Operational Domain. |  |
| Resource Exchange | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Resource Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF elements from the Resource Domain. |  |  |  |
| Service Exchange | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Service Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF Service elements. |  |  |  |
| Operational Control Flow | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Operational Control Flow if the ArchiMate Flow relates Business Processes that map to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action. |  |  |  |
| Function Control Flow | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Function Contro Flow if the ArchiMate Flow relates Technology Process, Technology Function, Application Process, Application Function, or Business Function that maps to Function + FunctionAction. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Dependency if the ArchiMate Flow relation does not map to Resource Exchange or Operational Exchange. |  |  |  |
| 8 | Influence | Dependency | Common rule, but ArchiMate name and strength map to UAF Dependency name ("name strength"). |  |
| 9 | Realization | Phases | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Phases if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Actual Enterprise Phase) with Capability or Goal. |  |
| Exhibits | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Exhibits if the Archimate Realization relates ArchiMate Workpackage or BusinessService with Capability. |  |  |  |
| Maps To Capability | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Maps To Capability if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Process with Capability. |  |  |  |
| Is Capable To Perform | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Is Capable To Perform if the ArchiMate Realization relates:ArchiMate Business Function to Business Service (the source and target are opposite than in ArchiMate).ArchiMate Plateau to Business Function. |  |  |  |
| Constrained Element | The ArchiMate Realization information is added into the Constrained Element property of the UAF Constraint if the ArchiMatre Realization relates ArchiMate Constraint with any other element. No relations are created. |  |  |  |
| Desires | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Desires if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Capability with Outcome. |  |  |  |
| Satisfy | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Satisfy if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Outcome with Goal. |  |  |  |
| Composition | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Composition if the ArchiMate Realization relates:ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Equipment or Business Actor.ArchiMate Communication Network to Path.ArchiMate Product (which maps to UAF Service) to Business Service. |  |  |  |
| Implements | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Implements if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Actor, Equipment, Plateau to Resource or Business Role. |  |  |  |
| Version Released | The ArchiMate Realization information is added to UAF Version Released tag if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Workpackage (which maps to UAF Actual Project and Actual Project Milestone) to Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) or Business Service. No relationship is created. |  |  |  |
| Annotated Element | The ArchiMate Realization information is added to the UAF Annotated Element tag if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Grouping. No relationship is created. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Realization relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 10 | Serving | Dependency | The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Serving relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |
| Supports | The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Supports if ArchiMate Business Service is related to Business Process using the ArchiMate Serving relation (UAF Service supports Operational Activity). |  |  |  |
| 11 | Specialization | Generalization | Common rule |  |
| Containment | The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Containment if the ArchiMate Specialization relation is between Requirements, Goals, and Principles.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Specialization relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 12 | Triggering | Operational Exchange | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Performers. |  |
| Resource Exchange | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Resource Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Resources. |  |  |  |
| Service Exchange | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Exchange when the Archimate Triggering is related to Services. |  |  |  |
| Operational Control Flow | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Activity Actions. |  |  |  |
| Function Control Flow | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Function Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Function Actions. |  |  |  |
| Service Control Flow | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Service Function Actions. |  |  |  |
| Project Sequence | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Project Sequence when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Projects. |  |  |  |
| Milestone Dependency | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Milestone Dependency when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Project Milestones. |  |  |  |
| Transition | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Transition when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to States. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Triggering relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |

195753002

Flow </td><td><p>The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Function Contro Flow if the ArchiMate Flow relates Technology Process, Technology Function, Application Process, Application Function, or Business Function that maps to Function + FunctionAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Dependency</p></td><td><p>The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Dependency if the ArchiMate Flow relation does not map to Resource Exchange or Operational Exchange.</p></td></tr><tr><td>8</td><td colspan="2">Influence</td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">Common rule, but ArchiMate name and strength map to UAF Dependency name (&quot;name strength&quot;).</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="12">9<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="2" rowspan="12"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Realization</span><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Phases</td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Phases if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Actual Enterprise Phase) with Capability or Goal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Exhibits</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Exhibits if the Archimate Realization relates ArchiMate Workpackage or BusinessService with Capability.</td></tr><tr><td>Maps To Capability</td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Maps To Capability if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Process with Capability.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Is Capable To Perform</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Is Capable To Perform if the ArchiMate Realization relates:</p><ul><li>ArchiMate Business Function to Business Service (the source and target are opposite than in ArchiMate).</li><li>ArchiMate Plateau to Business Function.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Constrained Element</span></td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization information is added into the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Constrained Element property of the UAF Constraint if the ArchiMatre Realization relates ArchiMate Constraint with any other element. No relations are created.</span></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Desires</span></td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Desires if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Capability with Outcome.</td></tr><tr><td>Satisfy</td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Satisfy if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Outcome with Goal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Composition</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Composition if the ArchiMate Realization relates:</p><ul><li>ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Equipment or Business Actor.</li><li>ArchiMate Communication Network to Path.</li><li>ArchiMate Product (which maps to UAF Service) to Business Service.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Implements</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Implements if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Actor, Equipment, Plateau to Resource or Business Role.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Version Released</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization information is added to UAF Version Released tag if the ArchiMate Realization <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">relates ArchiMate  Workpackage (which maps to UAF Actual Project and Actual Project Milestone) to Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) or Business Service. No relationship is created.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Annotated Element</span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The ArchiMate Realization information is added to the UAF Annotated Element tag if the ArchiMate Realization</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Grouping. No relationship is created.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Realization relation import and mapping rule applies.</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">10</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Serving <br /><br /></td><td>Dependency</td><td>The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Serving relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Supports </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Supports if ArchiMate Business Service is related to Business Process using the ArchiMate Serving relation (UAF Service supports Operational Activity).</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">11</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="3">Specialization</td><td>Generalization</td><td>Common rule</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Containment</td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Containment if the ArchiMate Specialization relation is between Requirements, Goals, and Principles.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Specialization relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="10">12<br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="2" rowspan="10">Triggering<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Operational Exchange </td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Performers.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Exchange </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Resource Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Resources.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Exchange </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Exchange when the Archimate Triggering is related to Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Control Flow </td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Activity Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Function Control Flow </td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Function Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Control Flow </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Project Sequence </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Project Sequence when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Projects.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Milestone Dependency </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Milestone Dependency when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Project Milestones.</td></tr><tr><td>Transition</td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Transition when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to States.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Triggering relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4f4222a-38a0-4bba-8036-6487dce2b7c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">195753002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element mapping" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Mapping issues" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929250 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00925: Removed elements

- page_id: `191929250`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929250/Removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-12T12:06:20.531+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration > Elements mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All removed elements are no longer used in UAF 1.1 and don't map to the other elements.

If the element property or relationship does not exists in UAF 1.1, the additional information is added into the migrated element's To Do property. For more information about how to use To Do property, see in the page [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using To Do property' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

| UPDM 2.1 |
| --- |
| Activity Part Of Project |
| Activity Subject |
| Asynchronous Message |
| Association Of Information |
| Competence Provider |
| Competence Requirer |
| Condition Kind |
| Condition Property |
| Condition Type |
| Contract |
| Design Rule |
| Desired State |
| Details |
| Entity Attribute |
| Entity Item |
| Entity Relationship |
| External Individual |
| External Type |
| External Tuple |
| External Tuple Type |
| Geo Political Extent |
| Known Resource |
| Mission |
| Ontology Reference |
| Operational Event Trace |
| Operational State |
| Overlap |
| Participant |
| Process |
| Project |
| Resource Event Trace |
| Resource State |
| Semantic |
| Semantic Attribute |
| Service Description |
| Service Feature |
| Service Function Edge |
| Service Interaction |
| Service Level Value |
| Service Message Handler |
| Standard Configuration |
| Stereotype Extension |
| Subject Of Operational State Machine |
| Transactional |
| Transactional Attribute |
| View |
| Viewpoint |
| Wrapper |
| Wrapper Attribute |
